title: TrainsPRO
author: TrainsPRO
description: TrainsPRO features articles on railroad industry news and analysis, covering areas such as freight rail, passenger rail, mechanical, maintenance of way and regulatory updates. title: Vivid Ventures
author: Trevor Jensen
description:
Most contractors don’t struggle because they’re bad at their trade, they struggle because they’ve never been taught how to build a business.

On Vivid Ventures, Trevor and Matt share practical, no-fluff advice to help contractors and entrepreneurs scale with confidence. Each episode covers the systems, leadership, hiring, marketing, finances, mindset, and operational strategies that transform hardworking technicians into successful business owners.

Featuring conversations with industry leaders, successful entrepreneurs, and experts from across the construction world, Vivid Ventures is your resource for building a business that’s profitable, scalable, and built to last. If you’re ready to get out of the truck and start leading your company with intention, you’re in the right place.

title: Good Book?
author: Sam and Nick
description:
The podcast where two cousins who love to read and talk about books read books and talk about them.
Have you ever looked at a book and wondered if you should give it a try, or maybe just skip it? Have you ever wondered what the best rating system should be for books? Have you ever read a book and loved it so much and wanted to talk about it after but nobody else had read it so you try to make your friends read it and you even loan them your copy but then a year later when you ask about it they just return it without saying anything and you both avoid eye contact for a while? If so, welcome.

title: Stealing My Dad's Creative Real Estate Playbook
author: William Hawthorn
description: Podcast Title: Stealing My Dad's Creative Real Estate Playbook  Show Description:  What if you could bypass the "long, long, LONG educational runway" of traditional real estate and start generating gargantuan profits today?  Welcome to Stealing My Dad's Creative Real Estate Playbook—the raw, unfiltered, and practical podcast where a college professor daughter turns the tables on her father, Bill Hawthorn. After 30 years in the trenches, over $50 million in transactions, and the creation of the industry-leading Creative REI Reply software, Bill is finally handing over the keys to his entire empire.  In this series, we aren’t just talking theory. We’re pulling back the curtain on the FRED System (Flipping Real Estate Documents). You’ll learn how to follow the money, master the "Money Now, Money Monthly, Money Later" formula, and build a hands-off, automated real estate machine—all without using your own capital, credit, or risk.  What you’ll learn:      How to Own Nothing but Control Everything: Master the art of option agreements and take control of assets without the heavy burdens of ownership.      Anti-Negotiation Secrets: Learn the 12 magical words that stop the "tug-of-war" and get sellers to reveal their true motivations.      The Deal Hunting Machine: See how to leverage specialized AI agents to automate lead generation, skip-tracing, and offer submission while you sleep.      Real Estate Apprenticeship: Skip the "shiny objects" and get straight to the Standard Operating Procedures that turn rookies into high-performing Zealtors.  Whether you have a 400 credit score or a full-time job, Bill Hawthorn breaks down the exact steps to transform real estate from a high-stress headache into your own personal ATM.  Stop studying, start doing. Join us and steal the playbook that is changing the industry one deal at a time.  Subscribe now and start your journey to true financial freedom.

title: Aspects Of World
author: Jaison Prathish
description:
Welcome to Aspects of World — where we connect the dots between the forces shaping our future.
Every week, we dig into:
🤖 AI & Future — What's really happening in artificial intelligence, and what it means for the world ahead
💼 Business Case Studies — Real stories of companies that rose, fell, and pivoted — and the decisions behind them
🌍 Geopolitics — The power struggles, alliances, and conflicts driving global events
💰 Economics & Finance — The money movements behind the headlines
🧠 Psychology — The hidden biases and behaviors that drive markets, leaders, and everyday decisions
🚀 Productivity — Practical ways to think and work smarter in a fast-changing world
We believe most events aren't isolated — an AI breakthrough moves markets, reshapes power balances, and plays on human psychology, all at once. Aspects of World exists to show you those connections, so you understand what is happening and why it matters.
New videos regularly. Subscribe to see the world from every aspect.

title: Whispered Wonders Sleep
author: Whispered Wonders
description:
Have you ever been lying in bed, unable to sleep and found yourself wondering how birds know where to go in winter? Or what's actually at the bottom of the ocean?

That's what this podcast is for. Each episode, a calm voice sits with one of those quiet questions and slowly, gently unfolds it. There's no rush and nothing you need to remember. It's a bit like a bedtime story, except the story happens to be true.

Dim the lights, get comfortable, and let a little wonder carry you off to sleep. Episodes are about twenty minutes long, but you're welcome to fall asleep before the end. In fact, we hope you do.

New episodes weekly.

Whether it's her favorite book, or something she had to get through for school, you'll get her honest, kid-to- kid perspective on what's actually worth reading.
Every review ends with the ultimate verdict on the Official Bookshelf Scale. Will it get a full 5 out of 5 bookshelves, or will it be left gathering dust?
Tune in for your next great book recommendations!

title: Read-y or Not? With Becka and Tracy
author: readytoreadpodcast
description:
Read-y or Not? is a podcast where two teachers, Becka and Tracy, take a closer look at books through the lens of the classroom, child development, and real-life student emotions.
Each episode, we dive into a book and ask the big question: are kids ready for this one? We’ll talk themes, age-appropriateness, tough topics, classroom conversations, and whether a book belongs in the Never Read, Read Now, Read Later, or Read Together pile.
Whether you’re a teacher, parent, librarian, or lifelong book lover, join us for honest opinions, thoughtful discussion, and a little bookish chaos along the way.

title: Chit Chat and Kee Kee with Risha
author: Maurisha Ross-DeLaine
description:
Chit Chat & Kee Kee with Risha is your weekly group chat in podcast form.
We’re talking about real life stuff—friendship breakups, career moves, dating, motherhood, growth, and all the “what in the world is going on in my life” moments.
It’s honest. It’s a little messy. It’s funny. It’s reflective. And it always feels like you’re catching up with your homegirl over coffee, tea, or a little drinky drink—no judgment at all.
No experts. No perfection. Just real conversations, a little kee kee, and a whole lot of “girl… me too.”
Pull up a seat in the group chat—you’re always welcome here.

description: Embark on a hilarious, heart-pounding, and utterly relatable journey with hosts Amra and Lauren as they navigate the ups and downs (literally!) of life and the world of rock climbing. title: Hall Pass with Uriah Hall
author: Uriah Hall
description:
Hall Pass With Uriah Hall is a conversation-driven podcast hosted by former MMA star Uriah Hall that explores modern masculinity through honest, thought-provoking dialogue. Built on the idea that a "hall pass" gives you permission to go anywhere, the show embraces conversations without limits—using modern masculinity as the lens to explore the issues shaping today's world.

From relationships, mental health, success, business, politics, culture, fatherhood, identity, fitness, and personal growth to the biggest headlines and cultural debates, no topic is off limits. Every episode is an opportunity to challenge perspectives, ask better questions, and have the conversations most people avoid.

Known for his intensity inside the cage and humility outside of it, Uriah brings a unique perspective shaped by elite competition, discipline, resilience, and personal growth. Alongside influential guests from sports, entertainment, business, media, and beyond, Hall Pass goes beyond surface-level interviews to uncover the experiences, lessons, and beliefs that define modern life.

Whether you agree or disagree, every conversation gets a hall pass.

description: Welcome to The Forgotten Thrones TV Podcast, hosted by author James P. Patterson. Discover the rise and fall of ancient civilizations, legendary rulers, military strategy, leadership, and the timeless lessons hidden within history. From Sparta, Rome, Greece, Egypt, Persia, Assyria, Carthage, and Alexander the Great to forgotten empires rarely discussed, each episode connects the ancient world to modern life through immersive storytelling. Real History. Real Lessons. Timeless Impact.

title: It's Not Rocket Science
author: Oluwatobi Lawalson
description:
Nigeria's problems are complicated—but they're not unfixable

Here we break down Nigeria's biggest challenges and explore practical solutions that make sense.

Every episode we dig into:

The history behind the problem.
Why previous attempts have succeeded or failed.
The political, economic, and cultural realities that make change difficult.
Practical, realistic solutions that an ordinary Nigerian can understand and critique.

The goal isn't to pretend there's a magic fix. It's to start better conversations, challenge conventional thinking, and prove that solving problems begins with understanding them.

If you've ever looked at Nigeria and thought, "Surely there has to be a better way," you're in the right place.

New videos every week.

Because sometimes... it's not rocket science.

This is why she thought if she could just share people's stories and struggles and give advice from professionals, that she could still help people without them having to come inside an office. Inside these stories, will be laughing, crying and healing. Support this podcast: https://anchor.fm/lifestruggles/support

title: Bartleby, the Scrivener: A Story of Wall Street (The Power of "I Would Prefer Not To")
author: Herman Melville
description:
When a mysterious scrivener named Bartleby begins working in a Wall Street law office, his peculiar behavior sparks a profound existential crisis for his boss, an unnamed lawyer. Instead of completing tasks, Bartleby simply replies, "I would prefer not to." This simple phrase sets off a chain of events that delves deep into themes of isolation, free will, and the human condition. As the lawyer grapples with Bartleby's unyielding passivity, he finds himself questioning the very nature of work and purpose in a society that values productivity above all else.

In this thought-provoking novella, Herman Melville invites listeners to explore the complexities of modern life through the lens of one man’s quiet rebellion against the norms of his time. As Bartleby’s enigmatic presence disrupts the office, the lawyer's attempts to understand him lead to an emotional and moral reckoning. What lies beneath Bartleby’s stoic exterior? Why does he choose to withdraw from the world?

Prepare for a journey that challenges your perceptions of duty, empathy, and the impact of societal expectations. "Bartleby, the Scrivener: A Story of Wall Street" transcends its 19th-century setting, offering timeless insights that resonate today. With each twist and turn, this audiobook reveals the power of choice and the consequences of indifference, leaving you pondering: what does it truly mean to live?

title: The House on the Cliff: A Hardy Boys Adventure
author: Franklin W. Dixon
description:
When Frank and Joe Hardy stumble upon a rumored haunted house perched precariously on a cliff, their curiosity ignites a thrilling investigation that leads them into a world of danger and deception. As the brothers dig deeper, they uncover a sinister smuggling operation that stretches far beyond ghost stories. With their father, the renowned detective Fenton Hardy, guiding them, the boys must navigate through a web of mystery, encountering kidnappers, a shadowy figure lurking in the darkness, and unsettling secrets hidden within the house’s walls.

In "The House on the Cliff," adventure and suspense intertwine as the Hardy Boys face their most daunting challenge yet. Will they unravel the truth behind the haunted house before it claims another victim? Listeners will be drawn into a captivating tale that explores themes of bravery, brotherhood, and the relentless pursuit of justice.

Perfect for fans of action-packed mysteries, this second installment of The Hardy Boys series not only showcases the cleverness of Frank and Joe but also highlights the timeless appeal of youthful adventure. Join them on a journey filled with unexpected twists and nail-biting moments that will keep you guessing until the very end. Dive into the excitement, and let the Hardy Boys lead you through their most enigmatic case yet!

With a stable internet connection, viewers can access their favorite content whenever and wherever they choose. One of the biggest advantages of IPTV is the ability to stream content in high quality while enjoying features like video-on-demand, catch-up TV, and multi-device compatibility. A reliable IPTV provider should also offer smooth performance, responsive customer support, and an easy setup process to create a better viewing experience. Data centers went from the most boring buildings on Earth to the center of the global economy. Fifty billion dollar campuses, gigawatt power deals, bond markets straining to feed the machine, and towns fighting back one moratorium at a time.

description:
The Legacy Shift is a podcast for conscious mothers who know better, care deeply, and still find that when knowing isn’t enough, stress takes over.
This show reframes reactive moments not as failures of effort or mindset, but as nervous system capacity issues. We explore why calm can’t be forced, why consistency comes from safety, and why real change happens through embodiment—not self-monitoring or doing it right.
No fixing. No pressure. Just grounded conversations that make change possible without shame.
Follow me on IG - @patriciaabdallah_

title: Somewhere Over The Painbow
author: Somewhere Over The Painbow
description: Somewhere Over the Painbow is a father/daughter podcast hosted by two people with questionable coping skills and a lifetime of family chaos. We talk addiction, homelessness, dysfunction, survival, healing, bad decisions, and the hilarious moments. If you’ve got daddy issues, generational trauma & drama, or a sense of humor, we’re here to make you laugh, maybe cry, and remind you you’re not alone. Hosted by Moldy & Tote. Laugh now. Heal later.

title: WinAZ
author: winazcncom
description: WinAZ - Các trò chơi trực tuyến không chỉ mang lại niềm vui mà còn là một phương tiện giải trí thư giãn sau những giờ làm việc căng thẳng. Mọi người có thể tìm thấy các trò chơi phù hợp với sở thích cá nhân, từ các trò chơi yêu cầu sự tư duy logic đến những trò chơi đòi hỏi người chơi phải phản ứng nhanh nhạy. Với giao diện dễ sử dụng và các lựa chọn hấp dẫn, mọi người đều có thể dễ dàng tham gia vào những hoạt động giải trí này. WEB: https://winaz.cn.com/

title: The SCV Law and Life Podcast
author: Mason Rashtian
description: The SCV Law & Life Podcast is where legal insight meets real life. Hosted by Mason Rashtian in Santa Clarita Valley, this podcast goes beyond legal jargon to bring you practical conversations with attorneys, business professionals, and community leaders. Each episode explores topics that impact your daily life, from legal strategies and business challenges to personal growth and success stories.

title: Sincerely, Divorced
author: Bethany Pace & Hillary Livingston
description:
We're besties on a mission to actively disrupt the narrative of failure and isolation of divorce for those contemplating it, in the midst of it and those finding their way after it. Essentially, we—along with the thousands of other members of our community—are the ride or dies you didn't know you had.
By pioneering a modern dialogue around divorce and the end of long-term partnerships, we are changing the way people experience loss and grief, and helping them embrace the bold idea that their happiest days are ahead. Follow us on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook @sincerelydivorced for more.

title: AI Made simple with carmen
author: Carmen Skipworth
description:
AI Made Simple is the podcast for emerging leaders and entrepreneurs who know AI matters, but feel overwhelmed by the noise.
Hosted by Carmen Skipworth, this show cuts through fear-based headlines, technical jargon, and fluffy inspiration to bring you clear, practical, human-led conversations about artificial intelligence.
You don't need to be technical. You don't need to move at tech speed. And you don't need to feel behind.
Each episode helps you understand AI in a way that feels calm, accessible, and actionable, so you can confidently partner with it in your life and business.
This isn't about AI replacing humans. It's about humans leading with clarity while using AI as a powerful ally.
If you're ready to stop feeling overwhelmed and start feeling equipped, you're in the right place.
AI Made Simple. Human-Led. AI-Powered.

title: The Man's Playbook
author: Kenny & Tommy
description: A Man's Playbook

title: AIE Technology and AI Podcast
author: AIEMusic
description: This is the new podcast from AIE focussing on short capsules of technology and artificial intelligence especially focussed on South Asia as part of global trends affecting us all.

title: Immigrant Dad Podcast
author: immigrantdadpodcast
description:
Immigrant Dad Podcast is a public humanities podcast about migration, fatherhood, and the stories we carry with us.
Born out of my doctoral research on migration, this podcast is my way of centering immigrants’ own narratives, challenging dominant narratives about migration, and taking discourse on migration outside the academy and into the public. In each episode, I sit down with immigrant fathers from different backgrounds to talk about their journeys, the lives they’ve built, and the hopes they have for themselves and their children.
These conversations explore questions of identity, belonging, education, family, and home. They are honest, thoughtful, and deeply personal. While every story is unique, together they reveal what it means to navigate fatherhood across borders and cultures.
Whether you’re an immigrant, the child of immigrants, a parent, or simply someone who enjoys listening to people’s stories, I hope you’ll find something here that resonates with you.

title: Longevity For Dogs
author: The Dog Aging Institute
description:
Longevity for Dogs explores the science of canine health and aging. Join us as we discuss the cutting-edge science behind canine health and aging, from breakthrough research to practical insights that could transform your dog's future. Whether you're curious about the biology of aging or eager to discover what's reshaping canine wellness, we're here to help you and your best friend thrive.
Learn more at dogaginginstitute.org

title: The Husky Howl Podcast
author: Chad Summers and Dr. Tiffany Hamilton
description: Peeling back the curtain to tell the stories behind the stories of Trussville City Schools. The Husky Howl Podcast brings you conversations with the students, educators, coaches, and community members who make TCS special.

title: The Chirp Report
author: Talk Crim to Me
description:
We're Ella and Ainsley — roommates at Harvard, co-founders of Talk Crim to Me, and now bringing you The Chirp Report. What started as two roommates chopping it up turned into a podcast built on real conversations, no script required.
Each episode, we sit with a guest — starting with our own Division One teammates and coaches — to chirp about locker room legends, roommate stories, and true stories you won't believe. We'll branch out to alumni next, tracing where their journeys led.
Tune in, chirp with us, and get to know the people behind the game.

title: Flask To The Past
author: GKE
description: 3 absolute units sit around and drink and talk history and other things

title: The Abbey Daw Podcast - become your own muse.
author: Abbey Daw
description:
What if the version of you that you've been hiding is the one the world actually needs? The Abbey Daw Podcast gives you real tools to transform your mind and move toward purpose, dreams, and authentic expression — drawn from Abbey's own lived experience, two decades of teaching, and vulnerability shared through storytelling. You'll laugh, cry, and feel everything in between. Because becoming your own muse starts with giving yourself permission to stop performing.


New episodes every week. Come as you are — pull up a seat.

title: What Happened to the McStays?
author: ABC News
description:
In a quiet suburb of San Diego County, a family of four seemingly vanishes into thin air. When investigators search their home, they find the family’s two beloved dogs unattended outside, rotting food left around the house and clothes strewn across the floor. Did the McStays leave of their own accord, or did something far more sinister happen?

A six-part series from 20/20 and ABC Audio, hosted by Ashan Singh. New episodes Tuesdays.

title: newton morning
author: Dilan
description: newton morning

title: The Crime Vault ⭐
author: OUMAYMA
description:
🎙️ Real crime. Real investigations. Real stories. Explore gripping true crime cases, detective investigations, and unsolved mysteries. New episodes every day.

Become a supporter of this podcast: https://www.spreaker.com/podcast/the-crime-vault--7187966/support.

title: One Night One Shot
author: The Journeymen Project
description:
Welcome to One Night One Shot! 
A TTRPG anthology series, where you can jump in any time! No marathon required.
Developed and Produced by The Journeymen Project

title: 90310 WellnessCast with Dr Clare
author: wellnesscast90310
description:
90310 WellnessCast — Episode 1
Intermittent Fasting: Does It really work?
Is fasting actually better than just eating less, or has wellness culture been overselling it? This week Dr. Clare digs into the real evidence — including a brand new systematic review — on weight loss, inflammation, and the GLP-1 comparison everyone’s making right now.
Full evidence-graded source list (every study, graded 1-6): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1yDL-9XxCk2AnRFPbHacFZcq2XM7K45YB6fcRXA1Ifnk/view
Want personalised guidance on building a sustainable eating approach? Get in touch: https://ben-99b6679a.base44.app/functions/intakeForm?episode=ep1-intermittent-fasting
Follow Dr. Clare @beautydoctor.cc and the centre @90310wellness
Got a topic you want covered or questions on this episode? Send them our way — future episodes are shaped by what you want to know.

title: Diplomatic Éirewaves
author: Diplomatic Éirewaves
description: Diplomatic Eirewaves is the official podcast of the Embassy of Ireland in Ethiopia, bringing listeners behind the scenes of diplomacy through conversations with ambassadors, policymakers, artists, changemakers, and emerging leaders. Each episode explores the people, ideas, and partnerships shaping Ireland’s relationship with Ethiopia, Africa, and the wider world, covering topics from peacebuilding and international cooperation to culture, education, and youth leadership. Through thoughtful and engaging discussions, the podcast offers fresh perspectives on the work of diplomacy and the issues t

title: Fight the good fight of the Faith.
author: Calvary Chapel Lockhart
description: Welcome to fight the good fight of the faith with Pastor Pete Arciniega Jr. Fight the good fight of the faith is a radio ministry of Calvary Chapel Lockhart, Texas. We teach the whole counsel of God from Genesis to Revelation, precept upon precept. We worship, we preach Christ, and we love seeing transformation and growth in the followers of Christ. To get more information on the church, including service times, go to our website at calvarylockhart.com

title: Behind Your Reviews: Sassy Service Industry Superhero
author: X
description:
Welcome to Behind Your Reviews, the ultimate audio defense line for local business owners and hardworking staff. Hosted by X, this is the show that says the exact things your favorite baristas, servers, and shop owners wish they could say out loud—but can't, because they have to stay professional.

We are skipping the boring star ratings to dive straight into the absolute wildest, most unhinged complaints left on the internet with a straight face. From customers blaming coffee shops for the weather to the sheer absurdity of petty internet trolls, we are serving up the reality check the comment section desperately needs.

We don't just stop at the 1-star haters. Stick around for our special bonus days where we tear into clueless influencers, look at the non-coffee side of the menu, and unpack the chaotic comedy of Wonky Wednesdays and Bizarre Blessings.

Short, casual, and a little spicy. Are these reviewers just deeply miserable people, or is something else going on? Subscribe now, keep your favorite local spots safe, and let’s laugh at some nonsense.

title: À la Maison -  ICI C'EST PARIS
author: Paris Saint-Germain
description:
Du 4 au 8 juillet, Ici C'est Paris La Maison a accueilli le Beyond Vision Atelier, un rendez-vous unique réunissant créateurs, designers, artistes et entrepreneurs autour de masterclass inspirantes.

Pour prolonger ces échanges, le Paris Saint-Germain vous invite à découvrir une série de 12 podcasts exclusifs. Animée par Jean-Baptiste Toussaint — fondateur de Tales From the Clicks et du podcast officiel du Paris Saint-Germain Où tu es nous sommes là — cette collection de conversations de 15 minutes vous plonge dans les parcours, les inspirations et les coulisses de celles et ceux qui façonnent la création d'aujourd'hui et de demain et dans ce lien intime qui les lie au PSG.

Douze épisodes, douze rencontres, pour explorer les histoires, les idées et les visions de talents qui repoussent les frontières de la créativité.

title: Deadpan Donna: A Gleefully Grumpy Podcast
author: Jessie Blair
description:
Deadpan Donna: A Gleefully Grumpy Podcast is a dry, funny, and thoughtful show about surviving modern life with humour, honesty, and a healthy suspicion of unnecessary nonsense.

Hosted and performed by Jessie Blair, the podcast features Jessie and her gleefully grumpy alter ego, Deadpan Donna, as they discuss stress, creativity, burnout, relationships, identity, and what it means to stay grounded in a complicated world.

Part comedy, part reflection, part emotional support goblin, this podcast is for people who are overwhelmed, overthinking, neurodivergent, creative, tired, curious, or simply done with pretending everything is fine.

Stay gleefully grumpy. It's quality control.

title: Oceans Transit Podcast
author: Oceans Transit
description:
The Oceans Transit Podcast explores the people, practices, and ideas shaping public transportation. Each episode examines transit safety, training, leadership, customer service, technology, compliance, and the everyday challenges facing transit professionals.

Produced by Oceans Transit, the podcast brings practical insight, industry intelligence, and real-world experience to operators, trainers, supervisors, managers, safety professionals, and transit leaders.

“The oceans let you travel the world—why not transit?”

title: Mind Your Thinker
author: Cori & Frank Arena
description:
At Mind Your Thinker, PLLC, we help high-performing minds and teams sharpen focus, strengthen relationships, and sustain meaningful success. Through psychiatry-informed strategies and organizational insights, we partner with athletes, leaders, couples, and families to build clarity, resilience, and momentum — in competition, in business, and in life.


Operating at the intersection of healthcare, organizational psychology, and performance, Mind Your Thinker uses evidence-based tools and people analytics to improve retention, engagement, and overall well-being. We create environments that are collaborative, psychologically safe, and efficient, honoring individual talents, gifts, and preferences while driving collective growth.


The first season of Mind Your Thinker is a little lagniappe -  conversations between myself and my grandfather, Frank Arena.  His mind would always ask my mind, "what do you think?"  He created a psychologically safe environment for our minds to have difficult conversations about money, healthcare, politics, careers, & family.  Such an environment is the foundation of Mind Your Thinker, PLLC, and one which is conducive to the growth of individuals, groups, and organizations.   

Our Priority: The Minds of People

title: Dream Believe Achieve
author: Maggie Reneé
description:
What does it actually take to become extraordinary?
Dream Believe Achieve is for dreamers, doers, and future legends working to become extraordinary.
Hosted by Juilliard-trained singer, creator, and entrepreneur Maggie Reneé, this show turns ambition, discipline, creativity, and real-life transformation into powerful perspective shifts you can actually use.
Through honest stories, hard-won lessons, and the Dream → Believe → Achieve framework, Maggie breaks down what it means to stop waiting for permission to become your best self and achieve the goals of your dreams.

title: Hired For Talent, Fired for Behavior
author: Q4 Solutions
description:
Unlock a smarter way to lead, communicate, and connect at work with Q4 Solutions’ podcast on the Dimensional® Model of Behavior™. Through real-world examples, expert insights, and practical strategies, each episode explores the difference between results at any cost “Q1” behavior and more effective, people-centered “Q4” leadership. Learn to recognize challenging behaviors, understand what drives them, navigate difficult situations, and foster healthier, high-performing teams and workplace cultures. 
Tune in each month for a new episode of Hired for Talent, Fired for Behavior.

title: Hematology Unscripted with Dr Joshua Richter
author: Decera Clinical Insights
description: In this series, Dr Joshua Richter gives expert updates for clinicians who treat blood cancers.

title: Own the Stutter
author: Ranya Agrawal
description: Own the Stutter is a podcast dedicated to stutter awareness and helping people who stutter build confidence. Hosted by Ranya, the podcast features personal insights, confidence-building tips, and inspiring stories of successful people who stutter. Through honest conversations and real-life examples, Own the Stutter encourages listeners to embrace their voice and speak confidently.

title: Shooter Ready?
author: Tactical Rex
description:
This podcast brings together my coaching, firearms instruction, and tactical training into one place — real lessons pulled directly from the work.
You’ll hear breakdowns of drills, teaching moments, leadership principles, and the mindset behind tactical readiness.
Whether you’re a new shooter, a developing leader, or someone committed to personal capability, these episodes give you the tools to build confidence, clarity, and competence.

title: The Joy Club Podcast
author: Emily McCarthy
description:
Welcome to The Joy Club Podcast — a space where creativity, entrepreneurship, storytelling, business, design, and joyful living come together.

Hosted by Emily McCarthy, this podcast goes beyond the surface to explore the experiences that shape us: the wins, the setbacks, the pivots, the relationships, and the unexpected moments that become part of our story.

Through thoughtful conversations with founders, creatives, industry leaders, and inspiring voices, we uncover what it really looks like to build a meaningful life and brand with intention.

Because joy isn’t just a feeling — it’s a way of living.

title: Dirty By 30
author: Candice & Jake
description: Welcome to Dirty by Thirty, by Candice and Jake -- a podcast about life up to 30, and everything that comes after that. We are two Bay Area natives sharing our stories and experiences as Asian Americans millenials living and working in San Francisco Bay Area. You’ll hear us rant about work, relationships, dirty laundry, passion projects, and cheetos. So if you’re an avid consumer of cheetos, please listen. On a serious note, if you’re just like us (navigating your 20s and dreading the upcoming 30s) give us a listen.

title: Groovy Tunes with Dell
author: Retired Inspired
description: Take a wild ride through the greatest hits and one-hit wonders of the 1960s and '70s. From the psychedelic sounds of the Woodstock era to the architects of the California sound, join us for the stories behind the songs that defined a generation. Subscribe for a weekly dose of musical nostalgia.

title: The Not That Political Pod
author: The Not That Late Show
description: A current news podcast hosted by Chicago comedians

title: Trauma through the lens of a wallflower.
author: Anonymous
description: Trauma is a real challenge to live with and heal. I share my story here as someone who’s suffered incredible trauma and the lessons I learned. I explore those lessons. I don’t have all the answers, however I like to think my journey is worth sharing and that even if it helps one person it’s successful.

title: Getting Clinical
author: Ratnaja Katneni MD
description:
Getting Clinical podcast is a medical podcast on topics in medicine, treatments and trends, from wellness to illness. 

First episode talks about kidney as the master regulator of balance, the aging kidney, what labs mean from creatinine, cystatin C to Klotho introduction. The episode ends with signature framework that spans this podcast Balance.Adaptation. Resilience.

title: Benchmark Canterbury
author: Benchmark Canterbury
description: Trying to find the solutions behind optimised human movement and holistic health. Speaking to industry leaders from various disciplines and ideologies around human movement, health and performance. Exploring various topics and providing impactful takeaways that you can use to improve your training and lifestyle.

title: Frances Whiting’s Humankind
author: The Courier-Mail
description:
Deep conversations. Extraordinary people. Human stories.
Frances Whiting is an award-winning journalist, best-selling author, and Australia’s longest-running Sunday newspaper columnist. For more than 30 years, she has shared stories of heartache, triumph, love, and loss. Now, her new podcast from The Courier-Mail and Sunday Mail takes you behind the scenes of her most candid interviews to reveal what it truly means to be human.
In this limited series, Frances Whiting's Humankind, she shares her favourite conversations with politicians, celebrities, performers, and everyday people with vital stories to tell. Listen in as Frances sits down with wildlife conservationist Robert Irwin, journalist Cheng Lei, Pub Choir’s Astrid Jorgensen, radio personality Robin Bailey, and a prominent Queensland police officer facing an unfathomable family tragedy.
This is a podcast that draws you in and makes you cry, laugh, and feel. These are deep conversations that will stay with you long after they end.
Hit follow to never miss an episode of Frances Whiting's Humankind.
Read Frances’s columns at couriermail.com.au/journalists/fran-whiting and features at qweekend.com.au.

title: Critical Critical History
author: A Teacher
description:
Have you ever thought, "That's not the history I learned"? Delivered in chapter form, this podcast explores how the teaching of US history has fundamentally changed, told through the eyes of a veteran educator. We aren’t here to change your mind, lecture you, or demand agreement. Instead, we are opening up an honest, grounded space to look at how history education shifted, moving from an approach that sought to unite us, to one that actively divides us.
Start with a chapter that interests you or scroll to the Preface on the bottom and listen to the fullness of the audiobook.

title: Easywork
author: Jon Cruz
description: Interviews with individuals who make hard work the easy work.  These individuals make the uncomfortable, comfortable.

title: PRAY UNTIL YOUR SITUATION SHIFTS!!!
author: Abolade Aanu
description:
There are seasons in life when words fail, strength runs out, and every natural solution seems insufficient. Yet, throughout Scripture, one truth remains constant: God responds to sincere, faith-filled prayers. This podcast was birthed from that conviction—that no situation is beyond God's power when His people pray without ceasing.

PRAY UNTIL YOUR SITUATION SHIFTS is a place of continual prayer, worship, intercession, and waiting on the Holy Spirit. Every session is led as the Lord gives utterance. There is no script to follow and no formula to master. Sometimes we will spend time praying in the Spirit. At other times, we will worship, declare the Word of God, engage in prophetic intercession, or pray through specific prayer points as the Holy Spirit leads.

The purpose of this podcast is not merely to inspire but to create an atmosphere where believers can join together in prayer, build spiritual stamina, grow in intimacy with God, and witness His transforming power.

Whether you are trusting God for healing, restoration, breakthrough, divine direction, financial provision, family reconciliation, spiritual revival, salvation of loved ones, emotional healing, deliverance, open doors, or simply a deeper relationship with Jesus Christ, this altar is open for you.

Prayer changes things because prayer connects us with the One who never changes.

Every episode is an invitation to come boldly before the throne of grace. You do not have to pray perfectly. You simply need to come with a heart that is surrendered to God and willing to remain in His presence until His will is established.

Many believers know how to pray occasionally but struggle to remain consistent. This podcast seeks to cultivate a lifestyle of prayer rather than occasional moments of prayer. The goal is to encourage spiritual discipline, strengthen faith, and remind every listener that persistent prayer is never wasted.

Some sessions may be filled with intense intercession. Others may focus on thanksgiving, worship, repentance, praying through Scripture, praying in tongues, spiritual warfare, or quiet waiting before the Lord. Every gathering is yielded to the leadership of the Holy Spirit.

You are welcome to join live or listen later. Whether you participate while commuting, working, studying, resting, or during your personal prayer time, believe that God is able to meet you exactly where you are.

As you listen, don't become a spectator. Pray along. Lift your voice. Declare God's promises. Worship Him wholeheartedly. Pray in the Spirit if you have received that grace. Let every session become a personal encounter with God rather than merely another podcast episode.

Our confidence is built upon the Word of God:

"Men ought always to pray, and not to faint." — Luke 18:1

"Pray without ceasing." — 1 Thessalonians 5:17

"The effective, fervent prayer of a righteous person avails much." — James 5:16

"Call unto Me, and I will answer thee, and show thee great and mighty things, which thou knowest not." — Jeremiah 33:3

These are not empty promises. They are invitations from a faithful God who still hears, answers, delivers, restores, and transforms lives.

This podcast is for anyone who refuses to give up. It is for those who will continue to stand in faith until mountains move, chains break, doors open, hearts are healed, prodigals return, destinies are restored, and the glory of God is revealed.

We believe that consistent prayer produces consistent transformation. Sometimes God changes our circumstances immediately. Sometimes He changes us while we wait. In every case, prayer draws us closer to Him and aligns us with His perfect will.

If these prayer sessions bless you, share them with your family, friends, church members, and anyone who needs encouragement to keep seeking God. Together, let us raise an altar of continual prayer that reaches homes, cities, and nations.

May every episode strengthen your faith, deepen your intimacy with Christ, sharpen your spiritual discernment, and remind you that no circumstance is too difficult for God.

Stay expectant.
Stay persistent.
Stay prayerful.

Don't stop praying.

Pray until your situation shifts.

title: Sister Club
author: Sister Club Media
description:
Shh… Mum can’t know we’re up past bedtime. 
Sister Club is our space for pop culture, politics, sociology, and a little sisterly bickering – hosted by real life sisters Monica and Sophia. Monica has domestic bliss and a thriving career, and Sophia is technically unemployed and figuring life out somewhere in Europe. Together, we’ve got all the bases covered and no shortage of opinions to share. 
Welcome to the Club! Membership is open and subscription is free.

title: Fierce Farewells
author: Jennie Komp and Charles Muir
description:
Fierce Farewells is an 8-episode podcast about what becomes possible when we refuse to let death be handled generically. Hosted by Jennie Komp and Charles Muir, it explores some farewells that were as fierce, intentional, and alive as the people they honored, why that changes everyone in the room, and why it's time we celebrate more of them! Because how we say goodbye matters. And it can bring us together... if we let it.
Fierce Farewells is a 3 Bird View Production

title: Non Trad
author: Christy Cabán & Miguel Cabán
description: From small-town Kentucky and the tropical shores of Puerto Rico to raising kids in the heart of NYC, wife-and-husband duo Christy and Miguel Cabán are deconstructing their religious pasts, challenging traditional norms, and writing their own rules for what it means to be equal partners. Join them on the Non Trad podcast for a hilarious, raw, and delightfully charming look at current events, modern marriage, and what it means to raise your kids—and yourself—outside the boundaries of tradition.

title: The Blackbeak's Bookshelf
author: Mackenzie T.
description: Welcome to the place where fantasy books take center stage! Join Mackenzie as she reviews, deep dives, and gabs about fantasy stories from every subgenre—from epic quests and grimdark to romantasy and beyond. Expect thoughtful discussions, hot takes, favorite characters, worldbuilding, and all the bookish thoughts that come with getting lost in magical worlds. Grab your favourite book and let's escape into fantasy together!

title: The KB Show
author: Katie
description:
Welcome to The KB Show—where nothing is off the table.


I'm Katie, and every week we're having honest conversations about faith, fitness, motherhood, healing, business, mindset, and everything in between. Some episodes are just me sharing the lessons I'm learning in real time. Other episodes feature incredible guests with stories, expertise, and perspectives that will challenge, encourage, and inspire you.


If you've ever felt like you're trying to build a strong body, grow your faith, heal from your past, chase big goals, or simply become the person God created you to be... you're in the right place.


This isn't about having all the answers. It's about asking better questions, having real conversations, and growing together.


So grab your coffee, throw on your headphones, and welcome to The KB Show.

title: The Mahogany Circle Podcast
author: themahoganycirclepodcast
description:
New podca

Hosted by Dr. Tabatha Russell and Dr. Dan Young, the Mahogany Circle Podcast is a dynamic platform where meaningful conversations spark growth, inspire leadership, and strengthen community. Through engaging discussions with thought leaders, entrepreneurs, educators, professionals, and changemakers, the podcast explores the ideas, experiences, and challenges that shape personal and professional success.

Each episode offers authentic stories, practical insights, and diverse perspectives on leadership, education, business, culture, wellness, and community impact. Dr. Russell and Dr. Young create a welcoming space where listeners can gain knowledge, discover new opportunities, and connect with conversations that matter.

Whether you're pursuing personal growth, professional excellence, or a deeper understanding of the world around you, the Mahogany Circle Podcast invites you to join the conversation, expand your perspective, and become part of a community committed to learning, empowerment, and positive change.

st weblog

title: Wet Fly Swing Fly Fishing Podcast
author: Dave Stewart
description: Dave from the Wet Fly Swing Fly Fishing Blog interviews the biggest names in fly fishing and fly tying today. He digs out all of the best fly fishing tips and tricks to help you understand how to choose the right gear, find fish, present the fly, discover new patterns and much more. Find out about new rods, lines, techniques, knots, tying tools and unheard of tips along with great stories about life and the times of some of the most knowledgeable guides, shop owners, and fisherman from around the world. Season 1 covers all things steelhead fishing. Better understand topics like spey casting, nymphing vs. swinging flies, intruders, finding fish, conservation and finding new waters. Come join us for the current season and share the stories and great memories told by amazing people.

title: The Catalyzers
author: Mark Russell
description:
Every original thinker, entrepreneur, and innovator can pinpoint it – the exact moment they changed things forever. But how did it happen? And why?

Each month, digital transformation expert, Mark Russell, speaks with true Catalyzers – the people who drive change in positive, profound and meaningful ways.

If you aspire to be courageous, to do things differently, or to change things for the better, this must-hear podcast will inspire you with stories and actionable takeaways so you, too, can become a Catalyzer.

title: Always Hangry
author: George Duran
description:
Food isn't complicated. People make it complicated.

Always Hangry is a funny, honest, slightly unhinged podcast where chef and TV personality George Duran calls out the weird, frustrating, and totally ridiculous ways we all behave around food.

This is not a recipe show. It's not precious. It's not trying to impress you. It's the kind of conversation you have after a couple of drinks, when somebody finally says the thing everyone else is thinking but nobody wants to say out loud.

Why does one gluten-free guest suddenly change an entire dinner party? Why are the Costco samples always the wrong thing? When did we stop trusting our instincts at a restaurant and start trusting whatever looked good on social media? Every episode grabs the everyday stuff — ordering, shopping, food trends, other people's eating habits — and pulls it apart with humor, honesty, and just enough chef perspective to actually make it land.

Episodes open loose and opinionated: George and a co-host working through rants, food news, real-life scenarios, and arguments that start small and end up somewhere much bigger than they should. The energy comes from the back-and-forth and the disagreements. Nothing is locked in.

And then the show goes deeper. Many episodes turn from the rant into a real conversation with someone who genuinely knows the subject — a chef, a scientist, a farmer, a writer, an insider — the kind of expert who can tell you whether the thing we've all been arguing about is actually true. Same honesty, same humor, just with someone in the room who has the receipts.

Most food content either teaches you something or tries to look perfect. Always Hangry does neither, and somehow does both. It's for anyone who has ever been annoyed ordering at a restaurant, confused by a menu, skeptical of a trend, or stuck in a situation where dinner quietly turned into a social negotiation.

New episodes weekly. Built to be watched, clipped, shared, and argued over.

Always Hangry is what happens when you stop pretending food has to be serious and start admitting it's actually kind of ridiculous.

Watch, subscribe, sign up for the newsletter, and t ell us who George should be arguing with next at alwayshangrypodcast.com.

title: The Dr. Sethi Show
author: Dr. Saurabh Sethi - MD MPH
description: The Dr. Sethi Show is hosted by Dr. Saurabh Sethi, a California-based, double board-certified gastroenterologist and hepatologist trained at AIIMS, Harvard, and Stanford. Each episode breaks down the latest science on gut health, liver health, nutrition, metabolism, longevity, and preventive medicine into practical, evidence-based advice. Learn how to improve digestion, reverse fatty liver, optimize your health, and live longer with trusted medical insights backed by research.

title: Red Letter Life Discipleship
author: LivingRock Ministries
description: Welcome to Red Letter Life Discipleship, a podcast to empower Christian disciples to make disciples. The podcast is sponsored by LivingRock Ministries and supported by LifeStone Ministries. Together we seek to glorify God by building Christian leaders' conviction and confidence to help transform people into the image of Christ through biblical resources and real-life application training.

title: Bare Metal Cyber Insights
author: Dr Jason Edwards
description:
Bare Metal Cyber Insight is the audio companion to the weekly Insight article series published every Tuesday in the Bare Metal Cyber newsletter.

Each episode takes one important cybersecurity topic and examines it beyond the headline. From emerging threats and major breaches to security strategy, artificial intelligence, risk, governance, technology, policy, and the cybersecurity workforce, Insight explains what is happening, why it matters, and what organizations and professionals should understand next.

Hosted by cybersecurity author and educator Dr. Jason Edwards, the podcast makes complex subjects accessible without oversimplifying them. Episodes combine practical analysis, historical context, technical explanation, and lessons for security leaders, defenders, students, and anyone working to better understand the changing cybersecurity landscape.

New episodes are based on the latest Bare Metal Cyber Insight articles.

title: The Content Box
author: Two Brothers Creative
description: The Content Box is your full-service content marketing solution. Stop feeling overwhelmed by digital marketing. Take control with simple solutions, systems, and tools we will share in each episode.  Hosted by Matt Tompkins and Tracy Winkler, a marketing and business coach who is truly passionate about helping you without expecting anything in return (except maybe for you to feel less stressed and set up for success!).

title: The Career Blueprint
author: Allison King
description:
The Career Blueprint is a career-focused podcast that introduces listeners to professionals from all walks of life. Through their stories, challenges, and advice, you'll gain insight into what different jobs are really like and discover possibilities you may have never considered.

Whether you're choosing a major, exploring career options, or simply looking for inspiration, this podcast is here to help you find a path that's right for you.

title: Historic reduction of poverty in Colombi
author: hamilton urbano
description: The podcast was created for the purpose of an academic activity.

title: Whose Mug is This?
author: Nick and Rosie
description: Dolly was right: living the 9-to-5 life is tough. So, you might as well laugh at it. Join us as we dive into weird jobs, ridiculous job titles, bizarre stories from the history of work and all the other nonsense that comes with earning a living. Expect silly games, strange trivia and plenty of pointless chatter from two people who have no idea what they're doing.

title: Pass The Rock: Real Talk
author: Brian Dailey & Laurence Willis
description: Pass the Rock!!! Meeting at the intersection of faith in Jesus and the sports we love. Laurence Willis & Brian Dailey will cover personal stories, daily devotionals and other content created to fuel the student-athlete, coach, parent or anyone else in their pursuit of their faith and athletic goals. Lessgo!!!

title: Daily  Message
author: Media Platform
description: Welcome to Daily Message podcast Network

title: The Examined Life: Philosophy for Real People
author: Dr. Scott M. Sullivan
description: The great books of the philosophical tradition — Aristotle, Cicero, Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and beyond — translated into plain language for serious people. No jargon, no self-help fluff. Just timeless wisdom for living well, from PhD philosopher Dr. Scott M. Sullivan. Vita Examinata.

title: Know Christ. Promote Human Dignity. Transform Society.
author: Archdiocese of Milwaukee
description: Now is the time to know Christ, promote human dignity, and transform society. Archbishop Grob and friends guide us through ways we can achieve this. Listen in.

title: The ShatteredTruth Podcast Show
author: Calvin Harrell
description:
Shattered Truth is a faith-focused broadcast dedicated to exploring Christian theology, spiritual warfare, biblical truth, and the cultural issues shaping our world. Through honest conversations, biblical examination, research, evidence-based resources, and the collaboration of diverse co-hosts and guests, the show seeks to equip believers with the knowledge, tools, and discernment needed to navigate deception and stand firmly in truth.
Shattered Truth goes beyond discussion by integrating the teachings and example of Jesus Christ into real-time living—demonstrating what it means to walk in faith, love others, pursue righteousness, and apply the Gospel to everyday life.
The ultimate mission is to shatter falsehood, expose deception, and reveal absolute truth through the authority of Scripture, the life and teachings of Christ, and a commitment to seeking truth wherever it leads.

title: No Commies in America
author: Karl Michael
description: If you’re suspicious that the Democratic Party has gone off the rails, you’ve come to the right place. Welcome to the No Commies in America Podcast. I am your host, Karl Michael. Here you’ll learn the truth about the deceivers who want you to believe Democratic Socialism is good. It is not. The real goal is to lead America towards Communism.

title: The Derrick Owens show
author: Derrick Owens
description: The Derrick Owens show is full of music talk and fun.

title: ZION CHURCH
author: ZION CHURCH
description:
Welcome to the Zion Church Podcast.


Each week, you’ll hear biblical teaching from Lead Pastor Jon Krist and members of the Zion Church teaching team, designed to help you know Jesus, grow in your faith, and live on mission.


At Zion Church in San Clemente, California, we exist to see the lost saved, the saved ignited, and the world changed. We believe Jesus is over everything, God’s presence changes everything, and His Word is the foundation for how we live.

Whether you’re looking for encouragement, practical biblical teaching, or a deeper relationship with Jesus, our prayer is that these messages help you encounter God and take your next step of faith.


Join us Sundays at 8:00AM, 9:30AM, and 11:00AM at 1201 Puerta Del Sol, San Clemente, CA.


For more information, visit zionchurch.com.

title: The Scarlet Plague - Unabridged
author: Dream audio books
description:
he Scarlet Plague by Jack London is a haunting post-apocalyptic novella that imagines the collapse of civilization after a deadly global epidemic. First published in 1912, the story takes place decades after the Scarlet Death has destroyed most of humanity. An elderly survivor, James Howard Smith, wanders through the ruins of the San Francisco Bay Area with his grandsons, attempting to preserve the memory of the vanished world.
Through his account, listeners discover how quickly social order, technology, education, and wealth disappeared when the plague spread. London combines science fiction, adventure, and social criticism to explore humanity’s vulnerability and the fragile foundations of civilization. The contrast between the old man’s knowledge and the children’s primitive existence gives the narrative both emotional power and historical depth.
This complete audiobook offers an accessible way to experience one of Jack London’s most imaginative works. It is ideal for listeners interested in classic science fiction, dystopian literature, survival stories, pandemics, and reflections on human progress. Listen online and enter a ruined future where knowledge struggles to survive alongside humanity. Its concise length, vivid imagery, and unsettling message make this prophetic tale especially compelling for both new readers and devoted classic-literature enthusiasts.
Presented by DreamAudiobooks.

title: Our Cause Podcast
author: Our Cause Podcast
description: Welcome to Our Cause: Social Action in Motion, the podcast that amplifies the impactful work of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Inc. Join us as we delve into the heart of our Social Action Program. Each episode features inspiring stories, insightful interviews, and actionable strategies from Sigma brothers and community leaders who are making a difference. From health advocacy and educational empowerment to political engagement and economic development, we cover a wide range of topics that highlight our commitment to social, political, and philanthropic leadership.

title: The Inner Compass with Sasha
author: Heal with Sasha
description:
Welcome to inner compass with Sasha- It is a place where you will find solitude and learn to create a path to discover your inner self for healing, growth, self discovery and expansion. 
There will be conversations to deal with challenges, emotional healing, mindset shifts , spiritual growth and transformation through relatable conversations. 
If you’ve ever felt lost, overwhelmed, or unsure of your next step, this podcast is here to remind you that your inner compass already knows the way.
✨ Together we heal, together we grow, together we rise.

title: Cargo Pants Podcast
author: Marlon Copeland
description:
Dads wear ’em.
Tech bros wear ’em.
Tinkerers wear ’em.
They fit almost any situation.
You wear them.
You can put anything in them.
Just like this podcast. Welcome. Hope you have a good listen.

title: The Michael Liebowitz Show
author: The Rational Alternative
description:
The Michael Liebowitz Show explores ideas from a rational egoist perspective - how individuals should act in their own rational self-interest to support their well-being, happiness, and long-term flourishing.
In a culture where altruism is often treated as the highest moral ideal, this show offers a different perspective. It challenges the belief that we should constantly place others before ourselves, and instead argues for a life guided by reason, personal values, and mutually beneficial human relationships.

title: The Finance Chief Podcast
author: Finance Chief
description:
What does it take to lead the financial and capital strategies at the world’s largest businesses? The Finance Chief Podcast, from Finance Chief, has the answers.
We speak with global heads of finance and senior C-suite executives, looking beyond the spreadsheets to dissect the intricacies of successful corporate strategy. Our guests discuss the full scope of modern finance operations from leadership and technology to business transformation, people, culture and capital strategy, to provide essential listening for financial leaders.
Discover more at www.financechief.com

title: Tecnover
author: Brian
description: Tecnover has spent over 40 years building professional spray painting equipment in Italy, from airless sprayers to HVLP turbines. This podcast explores the craftsmanship, innovation, and family history behind a brand trusted by professionals across five continents. Learn more at Tecnover.

title: The Secret History Of Sex
author: Wes Krueger
description:
**The Secret History of Sex** explores the fascinating, strange, and sometimes disturbing story of human sexuality throughout history. Each episode takes a documentary-style look at how sex, desire, power, relationships, fetishes, and taboos have shaped civilizations from the ancient world to modern times.
From the private lives of kings and queens to the hidden world of ancient brothels, from myths and legends to historical scandals, we separate fact from fiction using historical sources and modern research. Discover the origins of sexual customs, the evolution of human desire, and the event

title: From Yonder Shore
author: From Yonder Shore
description:
Step beyond the ordinary and tune into the unseen with Transmissions From Yonder Shore—a podcast for the mythic seeker at the liminal edge. Host Chris Kaplan guides you through poetic reflections, mythic conversations, and creative explorations with artists, astrologers, writers, and mystics. Each episode is an invitation to presence, magic, and the mysteries that move us. Join us for deep-dive interviews, solo meditations, and creative transmissions From Yonder Shore.
****
Website: fromyondershore.com
Newsletter: fromyondershore.com/hermits-lantern
Readings: fromyondershore.com/pathways-offerings
Instagram | YouTube: @fromyondershore

title: Charla 's Podcast
author: Charla
description: How you become successful by being motivated

title: The HexedVexed Experience: Radio Rituals
author: HexedVexed
description:
Welcome to the frequency where High Magic meets High Energy. 🎙️✨
Formerly the voice behind the legendary Sun Karma Radio, DJ DEMONESS is back to reclaim the airwaves—but this time, we’re casting a wider circle.
This show is the ultimate evolution of my broadcast history, blending the grit of my vintage archives with the spirit of the modern Occult and WitchTok communities. We are bridging the gap between the old-school radio days and the new-school spiritual revolution.
What to Expect:
The Vault: Rare, remastered interviews from the Sun Karma days (feat. icons like ”Ami Miller: The Killer Rap-stress”).
The New Era: Fresh, unfiltered conversations with today’s most powerful Witches, Tarot Readers, Rootworkers, and Spiritual Baddies.
True Style: We keep it HOT 🔥, we keep it INFORMATIVE 🧠, and we keep it FUN 😂.
This is a safe space for the magical and the misunderstood. Whether you are camera-shy or ready for your close-up, we are here to highlight your craft, not interrogate it.
Pull up a chair, grab your headphones, and maybe light a candle. The mic is hot.
Want to be a guest? I am currently curating a list of spiritual practitioners for upcoming episodes. 📧 Contact: DJDEMONESS@outlook.com

title: Hustle & Schmear's Podcast
author: Hustle & Schmear
description:
Welcome to Hustle & Schmear.

Hosted by Jason Salstein and Liz Cohen, this podcast is your dose of bagels, banter, and brutal honesty. Two besties yada, yada-ing, exploring, and diving deep into life’s curiosities. Whether you want to join or not, we don’t care, just let us know so we can have a head count for the wonton soup order!

title: Open to Receive
author: Anael Ivon
description:
**Open to Receive**
Hosted by Anael Ivon, Open to Receive explores feeling, familiarity, possibility, and what the mind and body recognize as real.
Through conversations on nervous system safety, prosperity, personal transformation, and the Field of Believability, each episode invites you to consider a different question:
What becomes possible when permission opens a gate?
Welcome to Open to Receive.

title: Virbank Voice: a Pokémon Podcast
author: Robbie
description: A lifelong Pokémon fan reflects on 30 years of adventures, memories, and nostalgia. Join me for a relaxed and personal journey through the world of Pokémon.

title: The Well Adjusted Chiropractor Podcast
author: Sarah Brodman
description: The Well Adjusted Chiropractor Podcast is where science meets meaningful conversation. Join Sarah Brodman, a chiropractic student, as she explores the nervous system, chiropractic, and today’s biggest health questions—while also diving into conversations about faith, personal growth, leadership, and life. Alongside chiropractors, healthcare professionals, students, and other inspiring guests, each episode is designed to educate and encourage you and your family to think differently about health, healing, and living well!

title: Narrative Decay
author: Lion Publishing
description:
What do you get when you combine two book nerds, a distaste for agenda-driven drivel, and a desire to return to better storytelling? Apparently, you get a publishing house and its flagship podcast!


On Narrative Decay, co-hosts Seth Cordle and Rebekah Edwards discuss the ideologically corrupted state of publishing, the decline of literacy in America, and how they're trying to change their little corner of the book world.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: damian's healing
author: damian
description: A collection of thoughts, emotions, and moments from my journey through life. This podcast is about healing, growing, loving, struggling, and finding meaning in the chaos. Not a guide, not a solution — just one person trying to understand life one thought at a time.

title: LifeTVnow
author: Joe Horn
description:
LifeTVnow is a Christ-centered program hosted by Joe Horn, a best-selling author, award-winning film producer, prolific child advocate, and board-certified Holistic Health Practitioner, alongside his wife, Katherine Horn, a holistic lifestyle advocate, and Jamie Woodle, an Integrative Nutrition Health Coach with a Master of Science in Health Education.
LifeTVnow explores a wide range of topics, including whole-body regenerative health—addressing the mind, body, spirit, and lifestyle—while highlighting the latest breakthroughs in naturally restoring health and reversing chronic disease. The program also examines Bible prophecy alongside the cultural, political, and socioeconomic issues shaping our daily lives, exploring truth, faith, freedom, family, and the challenges facing America and the world through a biblical worldview. From government overreach to medical autonomy, from spiritual warfare to personal sovereignty, LifeTVnow engages the issues that influence how we live, raise our families, steward our health, and navigate an increasingly complex world.

title: Ben Church of Christ
author: Ben Church of Christ
description:
These are lessons broadcast each Sunday @ 9:45 on KWOZ 103.3 in Northern Arkansas. They are provided by the Ben Church of Christ with host Jerry Prince. 
Sessions noted as Sermons are full lessons as presented as part of worship services from various speakers.
Visit Benchurchofchrist.org. 
Email - info@benchurchofchrist.org

title: Just Some Dudes Fantasy Football Show
author: Jason Pruitt
description: Celebrating the FAN in Fantasy! What happens when a bunch of average guys try to make a Fantasy Football podcast? We're all about to find out!

title: The Listening Ledger
author: Dawn
description: Real people. Real investing stories. The Listening Ledger is where everyday investors share their unfiltered wins, losses, and lessons — hosted by a genuinely curious listener who asks the questions you're already thinking.

title: Kozijnen Geleen
author: Jeroen Hendrickx
description: Kozijnen Geleen is dé podcast voor iedereen die meer wil weten over hoogwaardige raamkozijnen en de nieuwste ontwikkelingen binnen de branche. Als specialist in window frames delen wij praktische tips, inspirerende ideeën en deskundige informatie over materiaalkeuze, isolatie, duurzaamheid, onderhoud en design. Elke aflevering helpt huiseigenaren en bedrijven om beter geïnformeerde keuzes te maken voor hun woning of project. Met heldere uitleg en waardevolle inzichten laat Kozijnen Geleen zien hoe kwalitatieve kozijnen bijdragen aan comfort, energiezuinigheid en een stijlvolle uitstraling. Ontdek meer over onze expertise en blijf op de hoogte van de laatste trends via kozijnengeleen.com.

title: The Monk: A Romance (New Edition)
author: The Matthew Lewis
description:
Ambrosio, the renowned monk revered for his piety, finds his life spiraling into a dark abyss when he encounters a mysterious newcomer. This stranger, cloaked in secrets, ignites Ambrosio's hidden desires, leading him down a treacherous path of temptation and betrayal. As he grapples with his intense feelings for a beautiful novice, the boundaries between virtue and vice blur, revealing the sinister forces lurking within the monastery's walls.

In "Monk: A Romance," Matthew Lewis intricately weaves themes of forbidden love, supernatural intrigue, and moral decay. Listeners will embark on a gripping journey through the shadows of devotion, where every whispered prayer is overshadowed by the specter of evil. Ambrosio’s descent into darkness unfolds through a series of chilling events—each choice he makes pulls him deeper into a world where lust and ambition threaten to consume him.

With a cast of complex characters and subplots that explore the horrors of desire, betrayal, and the struggle between good and evil, this gothic tale reveals the devastating consequences of unrestrained passion. Will Ambrosio succumb to the shadowy temptations around him, or can he find redemption before it’s too late? Dive into this classic tale of gothic fiction, where every twist and turn keeps you on the edge of your seat.

title: Infinite Bridges Podcast
author: theasdproject
description: Welcome to Infinite Bridges, a student-led podcast by theasdproject dedicated to building connections, spreading awareness, and fostering a strong support system within the Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) community. This show features meaningful conversations with leading professionals, therapists, and passionate advocates focused entirely on ASD. Infinite Bridges serves as a platform to share valuable insights, uplift advocate voices, and provide community resources for families. Tune in for expert guidance as we work together to bridge the gaps in support for the ASD community.

title: Women Who Know Things
author: Erica Sloan & Kristine Elizabeth
description:
Hosted by Kristine Elizabeth and Erica Sloan, two lifelong friends exploring intuition, psychology, creativity, midlife, and the wisdom women earn through lived experience.


Women Who Know Things is a podcast that celebrates everyone who identifies as a woman, offering thoughtful conversations designed to inform, inspire, and empower.

title: Christine and Jesse Take Japan
author: Christine Pilkington
description:
This podcast is our travel guide for our two week trip in Japan.

Rather than flipping through guidebooks or searching the internet every morning, each episode tells the story of the day ahead: where we're going, why it matters, what to look for, what to eat, how to navigate, and a few details that might help us see Japan a little differently.


The goal isn't to check off attractions. It's to slow down, notice the details, understand the history and culture behind the places we're visiting, and make the most of every day.


Hopefully, years from now, these episodes will bring us right back to this trip.


Let's go to Japan. 🇯🇵

title: Grace Church STL
author: Grace Church STL
description: Our church podcast is all about sharing the incredible God stories of the amazing people right here in our congregation. By sharing these stories, we celebrate what God is doing in our church family and give everyone a reason to be inspired and proud of the people right here among us.

title: The 443: Letters to your Dream
author: Kwaku Osafo
description: Everyone has a dream. Kwaku goes on a journey to find those who dared to dream of making it in football. Dreams come true. Even when they're broken.

title: Believe New Things with Janine Durso
author: Janine Durso
description:
No matter how much dreaming or hustling you do, your life won't change until you believe something different is possible for you.

Here we'll explore how your beliefs shape your decisions, your actions, and ultimately your life. We'll question the stories you've accepted as truth, because some of them have been talking you out of what you really want.

Once you know how to change a belief, you can change far more than you ever imagined. Because it's not magic. It's a method.

I'm Janine Durso, The Believist®.

It's time to believe new things.

title: Reel Magnolias

description: A podcast about Louisiana at the Movies, the highs, the lows and the lagniappe!

description: The MAJRs podcast with Mikey, Antonio, Jahi, and Rece. We just here talking sports.
