author: Boarding Training Australia
description:
Welcome to the Boarding Bites Podcast — Evidence for Better Boarding, where we explore the latest educational research, social trends and industry developments shaping residential education.

Each episode, we examine one topic and ask three questions:  

What does the evidence say?  

Why does it matter for boarding?  

And what can we do differently tomorrow to build thriving residential communities?

title: The NeuroBlossom™ Podcast
author: NeuroBlossom™ Media
description: The NeuroBlossom™ Podcast explores the brain, the nervous system, and the science of human potential. Hosted by Dr. Marla Lerner-Powers, a chiropractor with a passion for nervous system optimization, it examines neuroscience, health, and resilience through the lens of a simple idea: each of us has a remarkable capacity for meaningful change.

title: Professionally Delusional
author: maggieciechoski
description: Professionally Delusional is the podcast for ambitious women who refuse to live an average life. Each week, you'll learn how to rewire your mindset, build unshakable confidence, overcome self-doubt, master discipline, and create lasting wealth through identity, neuroscience, entrepreneurship, and high-performance habits. Whether you're building a business, growing on TikTok Shop, or becoming the next version of yourself, you'll leave every episode with practical strategies and the belief that your biggest goals are possible. It's time to think bigger, execute harder, and become professionally delusional.

title: Leadership is a Verb
author: Mike Mock
description:
This podcast is about what leadership looks like when the pressure is real, the stakes are high, and people are counting on you. Through honest stories, practical lessons, and evidence-based ideas, each episode will give you tools you can use immediately.

Leadership is not a title. It is what you do next.

title: KM Test Account
author: Connor Reed
description: Testing for OpenClaw workflows.

title: The Living Pure Podcast
author: Marsha Brown
description: Living Pure is a podcast by Marsha Brown that explores authentic worship, healthy relationships, mental and spiritual well-being, and living out your God-given purpose. Through biblical insight, practical encouragement, original music, and reflections from Marsha’s books and blogs, each episode invites you to pursue wholeness in every area of life. Join us as we pursue pure faith, a renewed mind, meaningful relationships, purposeful living, and lasting hope, because God cares about every part of who you are.

title: Sathiavani Wellness Podcast
author: SATHIASEELAN N
description:
Sathiavani Wellness Podcast – Your Guide to Holistic Well-Being
Join the Sathiavani Wellness Podcast for expert insights on nutrition, weight management, fitness, mental health, and holistic well-being! Hosted by our family of certified wellness professionals, we offer science-backed guidance, practical tips, expert interviews, and real-life transformations. Take control of your health today!
🌿 Visit: Sathiavani.com
📺 Watch us: YouTube

title: PaperBag: The Roleplaying Game Show
author: ZealZaddy
description: PaperBag gets its name from elementary school kids drawing names from a bag to be teamed up with each other. GMing & improv performance collide in a chaotic crescendo of colossal proportions. Players compete for prizes by performing through scenes in random genres, guided by Game Masters pushing their performance to its highest level in a race against the clock. The bell is ringing, so class is starting!

title: What's New With You?
author: Scott Miller
description: Taking an in-depth look at how leaders are helping people in the community, with your host Scott Miller.

title: Living Faith Lee's Summit
author: Living Faith Lee's Summit
description: Living Faith is a church in Lee's Summit, Missouri committed to making disciples who will abide in Christ, be established in his Word, love one another and bear much fruit. Our long-term goal, by God's grace, is to become an equipping and sending church that transforms our communities, city and world with the gospel.

title: Healthy Teen Life
author: Leslie Rose | Life Coach for Teenagers and College Students
description: Healthy Teen Life is a weekly podcast providing teens, college-age young adults and their parents, with information, inspiration, and guidance on current teen health, nutrition, and mental wellness topics. Tap your podcast player icon below to listen now!

title: Rethinking Early Years with Laura Moore-Williams
author: Laura Moore-Williams
description:
Rethinking Early Years

Reflective conversations that challenge assumptions and strengthen Early Years practice.


A podcast for Early Years practitioners who want to reflect on their practice, question the things we’ve 'always done', and create environments where children can truly thrive.


Hosted by Early Years Consultant, Laura Moore-Williams, each short episode explores the questions, challenges and misconceptions that come up in settings all the time - from behaviour and continuous provision to outdoor learning, play and the role of the adult.


Through real experiences, evidence-informed insights and practical reflection, each episode offers ideas and small shifts that practitioners can take straight back into their own practice.

Meaningful change doesn’t always come from doing more. Sometimes, it comes from seeing things differently.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: (un)formed
author: Harold Smith and Scott Coulter
description: Unformed is a podcast about living a life of faith after deconstruction. Join your hosts Harold Smith and Scott Coulter for a new episode every other week. This series is all about what it means to live with a more open, loving, and expansive vision of the Christian Faith. We dive into all the questions you were told "not to ask", and push back on the false limits that have been put around God's Love.

title: Sound Dynamics Radio's Podcast
author: Sound Dynamics Radio
description: Vision is more than seeing the future—it is seeing life through God's eyes. Without vision, dreams fade, purpose is forgotten, and destiny remains undiscovered. In this powerful message, Apostle David Wale Feso reveals how divine vision gives direction in times of uncertainty, hope in moments of despair, and strength to keep moving forward. Let this life-changing teaching awaken your faith, renew your purpose, and inspire you to pursue the future God has prepared for you.

title: Once Upon A Game: True Sports Stories for Bedtime
author: Chris
description:
What if the greatest bedtime stories weren't fairy tales at all?


Here at Once Upon a Game, we believe the best stories are true stories—and the sports world has provided some of the best!


Tune in at bedtime—or anytime—to hear about some of the most unforgettable games, legendary athletes, and iconic moments in sports history.


Perfect for introducing young fans to the biggest moments in their favorite team's history—or reminding the young at heart why they first fell in love with sports—Once Upon a Game is filled with stories the whole family can enjoy together.


Real sporting events into exciting, family-friendly bedtime stories told with the wonder of a classic "once upon a time" fairytale. Each episode is filled with examples courage, perseverance, determination, belief, and teamwork. Incredible comebacks, and last-second victories that sometimes seem too amazing to be true take center stage.


Whether you're winding down before bed, listening on the go, or sharing your love of sports with the next generation, every episode begins the same way...

Once upon a game...

title: Live on Purpose Radio
author: Dr. Paul Jenkins
description: Live On Purpose Radio features principles of prosperity, abundance, and positive psychology to help you create and live the life you love. As you listen to Live On Purpose Radio, you’ll hear powerful stories of ordinary people doing extraordinary things. Dr. Paul Jenkins interviews inspiring people about principles that empower them to live on purpose. Living on purpose means that you have a purpose, and that you do it intentionally.  Live on Purpose Radio, LiveOnPurposeRadio.com, started in 2007 and has been helping listeners worldwide overcome the effects of noxious negativity with a regular dose of Pathological Positivity. Share Live On Purpose Radio with anyone you know who would appreciate a dose of positivity in their life. Visit DrPaulJenkins.com for more resources to Live On Purpose.

title: Missed My 2 Drop
author: Alexander Nguyen & Phi Nguyen
description: Riftbound! Riftbound news, updates, and discussions!

title: The Simmering Sessions
author: Nicole Reed
description: Welcome to The Simmering Sessions, where recipes are shared, stories are told, and every meal is an opportunity to bring people together. Join me as we explore home cooking, family traditions, kitchen tips, and heartfelt conversations that remind us food has a way of nourishing both body and soul. So pull up a chair, stay awhile, and let's create something worth sharing.

title: Leading Minds By Life Sciences Voice
author: Life Sciences Voice: Hosted by Jasmeet Sawhney
description:

title: At The Bar with The Chandlers
author: Douglas & Britt Chandler
description:
Twenty-seven years inside the legal system taught me one thing: the problems that destroy careers and fracture families almost never have to happen.
 
I’m Douglas Chandler. I’m the founder of Chandler Law in Alpharetta, Georgia. I got into this work because I spent the first half of my career suing lawyers for malpractice and litigating professional liability cases. 
What I kept finding, over and over, was that the thing that blew the whole situation up was almost always preventable. A cleaner document. A harder conversation. A few extra steps nobody took. 
Then those same lawyers (the ones I’d been suing) started coming back to hire me to defend them. Against malpractice claims. Against disciplinary complaints. Against ethics investigations. And I took that seriously, because they’d seen how I think.
But I also know this from the other side. I’ve hired estate planning attorneys for my own family. Twice. And both times, I walked out with a binder, a handshake, and almost no confidence that anything would actually work when it needed to. I know what it feels like to sit in that chair. That’s part of why this show exists.
My wife Britt has spent thirty years as a nurse practitioner working in high-risk, high-stakes clinical environments. Together, we sit down in our home bar (think Irish pub, think cozy, think the conversation you actually want to be having) and we talk through the real issues that affect law firm owners and business professionals across Georgia.
Follow us on LinkedIn, YouTube, Instagram, and Facebook because the best time to have this conversation is before you need to.

title: PR Hype Girl
author: Kate Haranis
description:
We’ve been told, “just be yourself.” But we know it’s not that simple.

Not every room was made for us, rewards our full selves, or has even earned the right to see us in all of our unadulterated glory.

Choosing how we show up isn’t being fake; it’s being thoughtful.

The thing is, stories we tell to the world (and to ourselves) shape how we move, what we believe and who we become.

Instead of defining “authenticity” as all or nothing, let's just learn to tell better stories. (And yes, I think it’s that easy.)

PR Hype Girl is the space for those of us who want to choose when to play the game and when to change it. Part strategy, part pep talk, part conversation with that new friend who will hype you up like it's last call at a college bar bathroom (and just happens to be a 20-year comms and reputation expert).

Take a deep breath, and let’s dive in!

title: Opportunity Intelligence Journal™
author: M.E. Crestwell Capital/Maxine Ebegbuzie
description:
The Value-Add Investor is now The Opportunity Intelligence Journal. In this first episode, Maxine Ebegbuzie explains why: what changed across months of research into valuation, underwriting, and portfolio decisions, and why property value alone stopped being the whole story.
She walks through a real CrestwellIQ property review, a building held by the same owner since the 1980s, with over 50% equity and a loan maturity approaching, that looked like a straightforward refinance on the surface. It wasn’t, once missing documentation and a deeper comparable analysis surfaced what the owner’s objective actually required. Plus, the three diagnostic questions worth running before any financing conversation: the owner’s true objective, the evidence behind the recommendation, and what an institutional underwriter would ask first.
In this episode:
Why this newsletter has a new name and a broader mission
A real property review where the obvious answer was the wrong one
Three questions to ask before any financing conversation
What’s coming: AVM cascades, the Five Layers of Opportunity Intelligence, Calculation Integrity, and more
New episodes every Wednesday morning. Read the companion piece at mecrestwellcapital.com/resources, or schedule a strategy conversation at mecrestwellcapital.com/contact.

title: Spiritual Deep Dive
author: Rabbi Derek "Dovid" Gormin
description:
Spiritual Deep Dive is dedicated to the thoughtful exploration of Penimiyus HaTorah - the inner dimension of spirituality. Through clear, practical, and inspiring learning, each episode seeks to uncover timeless wisdom that strengthens our relationship with The Almighty, deepens our understanding of ourselves, and enriches our daily lives.

Most episodes are approximately 10ish minutes, offering a word-by-word journey through foundational works of Jewish thought and Chassidus, together with practical applications for living a life of greater purpose, joy, and connection.

Whether you are taking your first steps into these profound teachings or have been learning them for years, our hope is that these conversations will inspire continued growth in Torah, Avodah, and Ahavas Yisroel.

Questions, comments, and feedback are always welcome at derek.gormin@gmail.com.

Please subscribe, share, and join us as we continue this journey together.


About Rabbi D. Gormin

Rabbi Derek "Dovid" Gormin is an educator, musician, speaker, and lifelong student of Torah with a passion for making the depth and beauty of Penimiyus HaTorah accessible and practical for contemporary life. He serves as Managing director for NCSY and Founding Dean of the JSU Global Campus. Rabbi Gormin lives in Los Angeles with his wife, Sarah Leah, and their five ridiculously cute children.


He believes that every person has infinite potential and that authentic Torah learning has the power to awaken that greatness within each of us.

title: Joyful in Jesus | Mindfulness, Transformation, Faith, Habit
author: Jeanette Finn - Joy Coach, Founder of Innocent Again, Author
description:
Do you ever feel like no matter how much you pray, serve, or give… it’s never enough? Do guilt, shame, and striving keep you running on empty? Maybe you’ve walked through heartbreak or loss, and now you wonder if real joy is even possible again. Do you long for peace that lasts — not just on Sundays, but right in the middle of your everyday life?
Welcome, sweet friend. Joyful in Jesus is your safe place to quiet the noise, heal your heart, and learn a simple, biblical way to renew your mind through God’s truth. Each episode will help you release guilt, silence negative self-talk, and reconnect with the deep peace and joy Jesus already purchased for you.
I’m Jeanette Finn — a woman of faith who’s walked through trauma, loss, and seasons where I felt unworthy and unseen. But God rebuilt my joy by transforming the way I think and the way I speak to myself. Now I’m here to share that same simple, life-giving process with you — rooted in His divine design and overflowing with grace.
If you’re ready to trade burnout and guilt for peace and purpose, you’re in the right place. Follow the show, and join me each week as we renew our minds, restore our peace, and rediscover joy — together, Joyful in Jesus. 💜

title: Geek Girls Story Salon
author: Jennifer L Giacalone
description: Writer and lifelong storyteller Jen Giacalone interviews creatives from across a variety of disciplines, from authors to actors to songwriters to filmmakers, about the art of storytelling.

description: A family of ttrpg players that navigate the crazy world of d and d

title: JaneWallStreet: At The Table
author: JaneWallStreet
description:
JaneWallStreet: At The Table is a podcast that explores the business and economics shaping the future of sports — through the lens of the women leading and driving it.
Hosted by Deirdre Lester, each episode brings together senior executives, operators, and investors for thoughtful, candid conversations on the decisions, strategies, and capital driving the industry forward. Focused on what’s actually working, what’s changing, and what comes next. From media and rights to ownership, investment, and growth, this is where perspective meets execution & where the business of sports looks different.

title: Extended Grace Church Plant Podcast
author: Vernon and Ashley Johnson
description: Extended Grace is the real-time, unfiltered story of Vernon and Ashley Johnson as they leave the life they knew to plant a church in South Charlotte, NC. Recorded as it happens, this podcast follows the calling, the doubts, and the everyday details of starting something from nothing. Whether you're following along, already part of the story, or dreaming of planting a church yourself one day, you'll get an honest look at what it really takes — the exciting parts and the hard ones. Learn more at extendedgracechurch.com.

title: Unmending Faith
author: Wanipa Ndhlovu
description:
I love God. I have questions. And that’s okay.
Unmending Faith is a space for faithful doubt and thoughtful belief. My name is Wanipa, and this podcast explores what it means to question deeply, hold tension, and revisit what we’ve always held about God, Scripture, and the church.
Through honest conversations and thoughtful reflection, it makes room for complexity, curiosity, and growth.
This is a journey without a guaranteed endpoint —but you’re welcome to come along.
Whether your faith feels uncertain, in-between, or even out of reach, you’re welcome here.

title: Outer Darkness Happy Hour Podcast
author: Outer Darkness Happy Hour Podcast
description:
Outer Darkness Happy Hour Podcast is where honest conversation meets storytelling over a drink. We explore life after belief, identity shifts, relationships, creativity, UFO talk, and rebuilding perspective while living in Utah and beyond. From faith transition to real-world experiences, nothing is off limits.
If you’re into faith deconstruction, Utah culture, UFO curiosity, storytelling, and honest talk about life and relationships, you’re in the right place.
Pour something, sit down, and join the conversation.

title: Wrinkles With Wisdom with David Lereah
author: David Lereah
description:
Wrinkles With Wisdom with David Lereah is a podcast about positive aging, purpose, resilience, connection, and joy.
Hosted by David Lereah, author of The Power of Positive Aging and founder of United We Age, this show explores the honest realities of growing older while celebrating the wisdom, humor, and strength that come with every stage of life.
Each week, David shares meaningful stories, expert insights, practical guidance, and heartfelt conversations about health, aging, loneliness, purpose, relationships, caregiving, gratitude, and the power of staying connected.
Positive aging does not mean pretending life is perfect. It means learning how to move forward with courage, curiosity, laughter, and dignity.
Because behind every wrinkle is a story, and every story still has so much more to offer.
Welcome to Wrinkles With Wisdom.

Designed for show pig enthusiasts, breeders, exhibitors, and families involved in the industry, The LineUp will feature conversations with industry leaders and innovators who are helping shape the future of the show livestock community. Along the way, the hosts will discuss current trends, share practical insights, tell stories from the road, and highlight the people who make the industry what it is.
Whether you're preparing for your next show season, looking to improve your program, or simply enjoy staying connected to the livestock industry, The LineUp aims to provide informative conversations, valuable perspectives, and a behind-the-scenes look at the people and ideas driving success.

If you’ve ever been forced to “process your feelings,” this one’s for you. Welcome to I Don't Wanna Talk To You. We're glad you're here—even if you didn't wanna be. Off Tha Record is where real conversations live. We talk life, culture, relationships, music, and everything in between — unfiltered and unapologetic. Nothing is scripted, nothing is sugarcoated. If it’s trending, controversial, inspiring, or personal — we’re speaking on it. This is the space where culture meets real life.

Tune in and immerse yourself in the genuine spirit of "Country Soul"!

title: The Holy Ghost Action Campaign Series
author: Charismatic Evangelistic Ministry
description: The Holy Ghost Action Campaign Series features powerful messages, worship, and life-changing moments from the annual Holy Ghost Action Campaign hosted by the Charismatic Evangelistic Ministry (CEM) in Accra, Ghana. Led by Rev. Steve Mensah and Rev. Eastwood Anaba, this revival is centered on prayer, the Word, worship, and the transforming power of the Holy Spirit. author: Phil Beastall
description: Life Had Other Ideas is a podcast exploring how ordinary people adapt when life doesn’t go to plan. Through honest, thoughtful conversations, guests share the experiences that shaped them, the challenges they faced, the lessons they learned, and the perspectives they’ve gained along the way. Every story is different, but each offers a glimpse into what it means to navigate the twists and turns of life.

title: Medical Practice Growth
author: John V
description: The show for interventional pain practices that want more consults and procedures on the schedule. I'm John Verdyan. I run paid patient acquisition for insurance-based clinics every single day: real budgets, real consults, real procedures booked. Here I break down what fills procedure days: ads built for pain patients, intake that screens out med-seekers, follow-up that carries patients through both diagnostic blocks, and reporting your practice can bill against. SCS, RFA, Intracept, Vertiflex, SI fusion. One problem per episode, 20 to 30 minutes. For pain practice owners and administrators.

title: I'd Love to Chat... with The Luvely Rae
author: The Luvely Rae
description: Welcome, let's have a chat. If you've ever had a chance to chat with The Luvely Rae at one of her New York City shows, then you may recall that she is always more than happy to discuss the little things, and as always to hear your thoughts. In this podcast The Luvely Rae (a rather lovely intellectual ecdysiast) invites you stay awhile for a brief conversation on the cultures and cultural changes. The thought provoking topics are meant to be conversation starters, and are provided akin to the popular weather girls of 1955 and the stimulating interludes of Gypsy Rose Lee. description: Every remarkable life has a story worth telling. Join host Sarah Beaupre as she explores the lives of artists, writers, musicians, world leaders, scientists, saints, and other fascinating people whose stories shaped our world. Through thoughtful storytelling and carefully researched narratives, Beau on Biography goes beyond the headlines to discover the human beings behind history—revealing the moments, relationships, and choices that continue to inspire us today. History gives us names, stories give us people.

title: The Mindset Generation
author: Adela Warkentin
description:
The world knows what they have achieved. This is who they really are.
Adela Warkentin, a sought-after mindset and executive coach, sits down with athletes, entrepreneurs, scientists, and thought leaders for conversations that reach past the résumé and into the person. Guests have included Bob Roth, CEO of the David Lynch Foundation, and Super Bowl champion Robert Turbin. Each episode moves beyond what they've achieved to explore who they are: the values that shaped them, the meaning behind what they built, and where they stand in life today. Together we explore human beyond achievement.

title: En ruta. Un viaje con quienes van de viaje.

El pódcast busca desmitificar la migración, rompiendo con las narrativas simplistas y estigmatizantes en el discurso público y mediático.

description:
Welcome to Balancing Unhinged with Amber Shah, the Shah of Saint Augustine.
Amber is a mother of two, Licensed Florida Realtor®, Registered Sivananda Yoga Teacher, Reiki Healer, NASM Certified Nutrition Coach, Personal Trainer, Group Exercise Instructor, author of Yoga in Sales, world traveler, trophy wife, and proudly unhinged elder millennial navigating life in the 21st century.
This podcast explores the intersection of real estate, wellness, motherhood, personal growth, spirituality, business, community, and everyday chaos. Join Amber now!

title: rebekah's Journey
author: rebekah's Journey
description: She left everything she knew—her family, her home, her future—to follow a divine calling she could not fully see. Rebekah's journey is one of faith, trust, and the courage to say "yes" when the path ahead was uncertain. Chosen to become the wife of Isaac and the mother of nations, her story reveals how God weaves ordinary lives into extraordinary purposes. From a simple act of kindness at a well to becoming a matriarch of Israel, Rebekah's steps were ordered by heaven. This episode explores her legacy of faith, her pivotal role in biblical history, and the power of choosing God's plan over comfort. Faith, divine appointment, biblical women, God's guidance, and the journey of obedience.

title: The Tech Side of Life
author: Soul Driven Studios
description: The Tech Side of Life is the podcast where technology meets humanity. Hosted by Akil Johnson and Jermaine Cobbs, we explore AI, emerging tech, productivity, creativity, communication, and digital culture through the lens of everyday life. Instead of chasing hype, we ask a simple question: does it actually make life better? Through insightful conversations, humor, and real-world stories, we examine how technology shapes the way we live, work, create, and connect. Technology gives us the topic. Humanity gives us the meaning.

title: The Archive

description: The Archive is a modern gallery where stories become memory stones. Conversations, testimonies, and real talk — all pointing back to Jesus. Because what the Lord has done is worth remembering.

The Brick Wall is the completely unscripted, poorly rehearsed and barely produced podcast of Media Thesis, a performance marketing agency that does media a whole lot better than they do content.
Hosted by Chris Sullivan and Bauer Fichter, each episode features candid conversations, sometimes with actual industry leaders, and the people building what’s next in marketing, media, AI, and business growth. The Brick Wall breaks through the real stories behind modern business success - the wins, the pivots, and the strategies that actually moved the needle.

Presented by ZAIDYN, the life sciences intelligence platform from ZS.

Whether you’re a lifelong resident, an expat, or just love the prefecture, tune in every month for sharp insights, upcoming local events, and the stories shaping life in the roof of Japan.

title: UNIT #522
author: UNIT #522
description: From bestselling author Jeffery Deaver comes "The Broken Window," a chilling thriller where a mastermind known only as "522" uses identity theft to frame innocent people for murder [citation:3][citation:4]. This episode explores the terrifying reality of a criminal who can ruin lives with a few keystrokes—planting false evidence, manipulating crime scenes, and making the system work against the innocent. When Lincoln Rhyme discovers the pattern, he realizes that "522" isn't just a number; it's a conspiracy that threatens everything he trusts. What if your identity was stolen—and used to make you a killer? Crime thriller, identity theft, forensic manipulation, Lincoln Rhyme, suspense, and the dark side of technology.

Hosted by the stoic, battle worn Ralyn Voss and his chaotic co-host Jasper "Ray Gun" Ray, each transmission blends frontier journalism, station gossip, strange encounters, and dark humor. Hunted by Core authorities but trusted by pilots, scavengers, and drifters alike. The Outbound Wave is a voice in the darkness, proof that no matter how far you roam, you're never truly alone.

title: Rare Kidney Source
author: BloodStream Media
description:
Your Podcast for Rare Kidney Conversations
Life with a rare kidney disease can be complex, isolating, and often misunderstood. Rare Kidney Source brings together voices from across the community to inform, inspire, and connect people living with rare kidney diseases such as C3 glomerulopathy (C3G) and primary immune-complex membranoproliferative glomerulonephritis (IC-MPGN). Through honest conversations and expert insights, we explore the realities of diagnosis, disease management, and everyday life to empower listeners with knowledge, perspective, and a sense of belonging to navigate their journey with greater confidence and support.
This podcast is intended for informational and educational purposes only and is not intended as medical advice. Always talk to your healthcare provider about questions you may have about the disease, its symptoms, and treatment.

Each week, Dr. Saurabh Sethi, Harvard and Stanford-trained gastroenterologist is here to simplify the science and make gut health practical. 
The show covers the latest research, shares strategies, and interviews world-class experts to give you doctor-approved advice for managing symptoms, improving digestion, and optimising overall wellness. 
You’ll learn how to make sense of food triggers, probiotics, microbiome tests, and everything in between. To give you clarity, confidence, and evidence-backed solutions you can actually use today.

Whether you're preparing for school exams, improving

Each week they unpack what it actually looks like to talk with God through honest conversation, personal stories, and the timeless truth of the Bible.

title: Mitchell Unlimited
author: ACE Radio
description:
The undisputed king of Melbourne current affairs is back, and this time, there are no limits.
Join iconic media veteran Neil Mitchell AO every week for Mitchell Unlimited, a brand-new podcast diving deep into the stories, headlines, and ideas shaping Australia.
Each week, Neil sits down with the nation’s most influential, fascinating, and entertaining public figures for the kind of sharp, unfiltered conversations he's famous for.
What to Expect Every Week:
•    Unrivaled Current Affairs: Deep dives into the issues that actually matter to Australians, backed by decades of trusted broadcasting experience.
•    Heavy-Hitting Guests: Exclusive, no-holds-barred chats with the country's biggest changemakers, thinkers, and entertainers.
•    'Father and Son' Segment: For a bit of fun, Neil is joined by his son, Cameron, for an entertaining, intergenerational look at modern Australian life. Expect contrasting perspectives, sharp wit, and a healthy dose of family debate.
From the biggest news stories to the dinner table debates, Mitchell Unlimited is your essential weekly audio briefing.

Content Unpacked is a brand-new long-form video podcast interview series presented by Content Marketing Mag and Niche Media. 
It features exclusive conversations with a prominent person from the creative, media, events, sports or arts space. 
The core idea is to understand how that individual and their team use content to promote a message/brand/idea across a diverse demographic. 
It will give content marketers a chance to learn directly from the people at the top of their fields, offering practical insights, creative solutions and lessons they can apply to their own work.

Short, useful episodes to help you make sense of working life and start taking action on whatever's got you worked up about work.

Welcome to the Naked Wellness podcast, hosted by nutritionist Mikaela Schifferle, where we talk about all things food freedom and nutrition. We cut through the ’BS’ information out there, debunk health myths, interview health experts and give you actionable steps to help you stop bingeing and become the best version of yourself.
If you’re interested in being a guest on the show, e-mail mikaela@undiet.co.

title: DJ Capo Oldies
author: DJ Capo
description:
Take a trip back to the soundtrack of your life with the greatest hits from the '50s, '60s, '70s, and beyond. From timeless rock & roll and soulful Motown to classic doo-wop, old-school R&B, and unforgettable slow jams, every episode is packed with the music that defined generations.

Whether you're reliving cherished memories or discovering these classics for the first time, you'll enjoy great music, fun stories, artist spotlights, and the nostalgia that only the oldies can bring.

Tune in, turn it up, and let the good times roll—because great music never gets old. 🎧 Listen, subscribe, and share. I also go live on TikTok where I take live dedications click on the link below

title: X-Minus One: A Binaural Podcast Meditation Series
author: Glenn Ostlund
description:
Originally broadcast on NBC from 1955 to 1958, X Minus One helped define the golden age of science fiction radio, adapting stories by legendary authors including Ray Bradbury, Isaac Asimov, Robert Heinlein, Philip K. Dick, and Murray Leinster. 

This collection reimagines those timeless adventures with a continuous 6 Hz theta binaural beat, inviting you into a deeply relaxed, immersive listening experience. As part of the 28-Day Nervous System Reset family of binaural meditations, these episodes encourage calm, imagination, and reflection. Headphones are required to experience the full binaural effect and support gentle theta brainwave entrainment.

author: Gabrielle Jehle
description: A podcast with Gabrielle Jehle & Friends. Connecting young adults with tools to network and succeed in their careers.

title: The Therapy Chronicles
author: Lynne Harford
description: The Therapy Chronicles is a bi-weekly podcast exploring the emotional experiences, patterns, and stories that shape our lives. Inspired by reflections from the therapy room and everyday life, each episode offers thoughtful conversations about healing, resilience, relationships, identity, and personal growth. Through reflections and guest conversations, we explore what it means to navigate change, trust ourselves, and live with greater self-awareness. A compassionate space for anyone curious about therapy, healing and the human experience.

title: Direct with Dana

Through thoughtful conversations, practical inspiration, and emotional storytelling, the podcast aims to encourage listeners to document their lives with intention, strengthen family bonds, and create keepsakes that grow more meaningful over time. Each episode features a working practitioner, researcher, or industry leader on the topics shaping the profession: market outlooks, retirement income, investor behavior, building and leading advisory teams, earning client trust, and the role of AI in advice. Past guests include Michael Kitces, Liz Ann Sonders, Mark Casady, John Bowen, and behavioral finance researcher Herman Brodie.
The show is built for advisors who already have the fundamentals and want the next layer of expertise — the kind that separates a competent advisor from an exceptional one. Expect practical conversations about serving clients with complex needs, standing out in a crowded market, and communicating your value in terms clients understand.
The podcast comes from the Institute that administers the CIMA®, CPWA®, and RMA® certifications, and it reflects the same standard: advanced, practitioner-focused, and grounded in the decisions advisors make with clients every day.

Most kids grew up with fairy tales; I grew up with nightmares. At seven, a headless torso was found in the river near my home. A year later, a neighboring town was rocked by a man who used axle grease to burn his wife's body.
For me, true crime wasn’t a trendy documentary subgenre—it was my childhood reality. As a Gen X kid, I wasn’t sheltered from real-world monsters.
Child of True Crime is a personal deep dive into local historical cases, unsolved murders, and the lasting trauma left behind. Ready to see the dark through the eyes of a child who never looked away? New episodes every Monday.

What is Revised Common Lectionary ?
The Revised Common Lectionary is a three-year cycle of weekly lections used to varying degrees by the vast majority of mainline Protestant churches in Canada and the United States. title: What The Peri? - With Kirsten Cooke
author: Kirsten Cooke
description:
You're not going crazy. You're not broken. And no — it's not "just stress."


Somewhere in your 40s or 50s, your body starts doing things nobody warned you about — the weight that won't shift, the broken sleep, the brain fog, the anxiety from nowhere, the feeling that you've lost yourself. And when you finally go looking for answers, too often you're dismissed, brushed off, or sent away still confused.


What the Peri? is the podcast for women navigating perimenopause and menopause who are done being dismissed and just need someone to make it all make sense. Each episode cuts through the noise, the myths and the medical gaslighting — with honest conversations, real science, and plain-English answers about your hormones, your symptoms, and what actually helps.


I'm Kirsten Cooke — a personal trainer, women's health coach, perimenopause and menopause coach, and mum of four. I'm not talking to you from the outside looking in; I'm walking this road myself. These are the conversations I wish someone had sat me down and had with me years ago.


You are not imagining it. It is not your fault. And you are not alone.


The change you didn't ask for. The conversations you need. 


Follow now so you don't miss an episode.

title: The Case Against Tyler Robinson
author: Bobby Capucci
description:
The Case Against Tyler Robinson: The Murder of Charlie Kirk is a comprehensive examination of the criminal case surrounding the killing of Charlie Kirk and the prosecution of the man accused of carrying it out. From the earliest stages of the investigation through the pretrial hearings and, ultimately, the courtroom battle itself, this podcast will follow the evidence wherever it leads. Each episode will break down the testimony, forensic findings, surveillance evidence, legal filings, witness accounts, investigative decisions, and defense challenges that shape the case against Tyler Robinson.

This will not be a story told through rumors, internet speculation, or predetermined conclusions. The focus will remain on the record: what prosecutors say they can prove, how the defense attempts to challenge that evidence, and what the court allows the jury to hear. When new evidence emerges, it will be examined carefully. When claims conflict, those contradictions will be explored. When questions remain unanswered, they will not be ignored or filled in with convenient assumptions.

The road to trial is rarely direct, especially in a case of this magnitude. There will be delays, motions, evidentiary disputes, strategic maneuvering, unexpected rulings, and developments that could change the direction of the case entirely. The prosecution will be forced to defend the strength of its evidence, while the defense will search for weaknesses, inconsistencies, procedural failures, and reasonable doubt. Every significant twist and turn will be followed, explained, and placed into the larger context of the case.

Along the way, the podcast will examine the physical and digital evidence, the timeline of events, the alleged movements of the accused, the handling of forensic material, the statements attributed to those involved, and the decisions made by investigators and prosecutors. It will also look closely at the legal strategies being used on both sides, including efforts to suppress evidence, challenge expert testimony, limit what can be presented at trial, and shape how the jury ultimately understands the case.

Most importantly, The Case Against Tyler Robinson will remain grounded in the principle that the evidence must lead the story, not the other way around. The purpose is not to manufacture certainty before the trial has run its course. It is to document the process honestly, scrutinize every major claim, and provide listeners with a clear understanding of what is happening inside and outside the courtroom.

As the case develops, the narrative may change. Evidence may become stronger, weaker, or more complicated than it first appeared. Witnesses may offer new information. Legal rulings may reshape the prosecution’s case or the defense’s response. This podcast will follow those developments in real time, separating established facts from contested claims and courtroom evidence from online speculation.

From the opening arguments to the final verdict, The Case Against Tyler Robinson: The Murder of Charlie Kirk will track one of the most closely watched criminal proceedings in the country. Wherever the evidence goes, wherever the trial leads, and whatever the final outcome may be, this podcast will be there to document it.

title: The Monday After
author: Darren Carlson
description:
The Monday After is a weekly reflection from Darren Carlson—pastor, missionary trainer, and writer—on the ordinary, unglamorous work of following Christ when the crowds have gone home and the week begins again.

Each episode moves through a simple rhythm: an honest devotional that doesn't flinch from hard truths, a story of God quietly at work in everyday encounters, and something worth reading to carry you through the week. Expect spare, declarative reflection rooted in Scripture, shaped by the local church, and aimed at the long obedience of staying, showing up, and being known.

For anyone who suspects the real test of faith isn't Sunday's high but Monday's grind.

author: JohnWallStreet
description: JohnWallStreet's The Sports Advisors Podcast, is a bi-weekly roundtable discussion of major issues impacting the business of sports by some of the sharpest minds in Sports. This is podcast for decision-makers hosted by genuine peers talking about the stories that actually matter and their second-order effects. title: Got Therapy?
author: Got Therapy?
description:
What is self-esteem, really? In this episode of Got Therapy?, Dr. Michael Baltimore and Dr. Dan Rose reconsider self-esteem through a psychodynamic lens — not as a high score you defend, but as the acceptance of a complex, often conflicted self.


The conversation moves through not-me states (the parts of ourselves we'd rather not own, from Sullivan and Bromberg), self-compassion, and why a rigid, always-positive self becomes brittle. Dan walks through normative narcissism after Kohut — why everyone feels a flash of "I don't measure up" driving past the neighbor's new car — the spectrum from healthy-level to disordered narcissism, and how mirroring and validation open the door to real change. Along the way: microtrauma and social comparison, holding a relationship with someone whose views you can't stand, and Fromm's question of having vs. being — the thread they carry into next week's "Cultivate Life."


Chapters and timestamps are in the pinned comment.


Got Therapy? makes complex psychological concepts accessible and practically applicable. New episodes weekly.

Through founder reflections, practical insights, and embodiment-based conversations, Temple Maison invites listeners to move beyond performance and reconnect with the person they are becoming.
This series serves as an introduction to the philosophy behind Temple Maison where ambition meets presence, creation meets embodiment, and success begins from within.

When Hitler invaded the Soviet Union in 1941, eleven-year-old Yasha’s mother made a decision that would save generations to come—the decision to flee. 

​​​​

What followed was an odyssey spanning thousands of miles in the shadow of two of history’s most brutal regimes.​​ In 2008, Yasha sat with his grandson, Jeremy, who recorded on an old camcorder as he told his story.

title: The Well Fucked Woman
author: Tanja Diamond $ Suzy Easterling
description:
Most podcasts want to make you comfortable.We're aiming for slightly aroused, mildly offended, intellectually stimulated, and wondering why nobody has ever said this out loud before.

Welcome to The Well-Fucked Woman.Hosted by Tanja Diamond and Suzy Easterling, this is where we drag the conversations everyone else politely steps around into the middle of the room and refuse to let them leave until they've answered a few uncomfortable questions.

Why do women know more about anti-aging creams than their own clitoris?

Why are we expected to instinctively know how to have extraordinary relationships when most of us never saw one growing up?

Why does everyone tell us to communicate better, yet nobody teaches us what we're actually afraid of saying?

Why are women still carrying shame that isn't even theirs?

Some weeks we'll be talking about sex. Other weeks it'll be betrayal, menopause, nervous systems, porn, body image, grief, love, desire, motherhood, trauma, friendship, aging, emotional intelligence, or the strange little lies that quietly run our lives while we're busy pretending we're fine.

We do messy, curiosity, brutal honesty, science, inappropriate laughter, and the occasional sentence that makes one of us stop mid-conversation and say, "Well... that's going to piss somebody off."

Excellent. The best conversations usually do.

So pull up a chair, pour yourself something delicious, and join us for the conversations you've been having in your own head for years but weren't quite sure you were allowed to say out loud.

One small warning before you hit play: we swear, we laugh loudly, we talk openly about sex, and we have an unhealthy relationship with polite society. If you're listening around children, coworkers, or anyone who still whispers the word "vagina," do yourself a favour and grab your headphones.

description:

Each episode will give an in-depth look at different aspects of Nuka, with commentary from the people who helped build the system and keep it operating to a high standard of quality today. We hope you will appreciate the insight into the inner workings of a system that has been nationally and internationally recognized for providing high-quality health care.
 
Podcast music is provided by Polly Napiryuk Andrews, Cup'ik from the community of Chevak, Alaska. The song Niitaqamaa Wii (When I Hear) is sung in Yup'ik, and translates to "When I hear the voices of my ancestors, it's beautiful, and it gives me flight". This song is composed by Ossie Kairaiuak, rendition by Polly Andrews, with drums by Michael Ulroan. Polly is a Learning and Development Advisor with Southcentral Foundation Family Wellness Warriors, a movement that focuses on Alaska Native culture and strengths to end cycles of trauma.

It lives inside the membership first, so members can get to know each other before they ever shake hands. Walk into the room already knowing who you want to meet. The conversations our guests choose to share, we release here for the whole city.

From culture wars to constitutional debates, from grassroots movements to national leadership, Mark Alford brings you strong conservative perspectives and thoughtful discussions that challenge mainstream narratives and spark meaningful reflection for the next generation of Making America Great Again.

Whether you’re navigating high-stakes business pivots, managing organizational culture, or trying to reclaim your presence at home, Dr. Traci offers the "Operational Blueprint" you need to lead with sustainable clarity.
Because leadership is not forged in comfort - it is forged in responsibility, awareness, and follow-through. 
Press play to stop "powering through" and start leading from a place of regulated, unshakeable confidence.
And remember, we've got your six!

This episode dives into the hidden world of business—where ambition meets betrayal, and fortunes are made or lost in a single decision. From corporate fraud to hostile takeovers, we uncover the strategies that built billion-dollar brands and the scandals that brought them crashing down. Join host Kevin Matteson (PhD in Ecology) and his adventuring party—Mike (Forever DM & Science Teacher), Rachel (Climate Educator), and Cassie (Wildlife Expert)—as they bridge the gap between tabletop roleplaying games and real-world science.


Each expedition, the party tracks down a classic fantasy creature to dissect its fictional ecology and connect it to real-world biology and conservation. How would a Gelatinous Cube actually digest its prey? What real-world fungal networks explain the Myconids?


Whether you are a Dungeon Master looking for richer world-building, a science educator searching for engaging classroom hooks, or a nature lover who enjoys nerd culture, pull up a chair at our tavern.

Grab your dice and your field journal. Keep exploring, because the real world is magical too!

Welcome to Soulflowered: Sacred Rest, a place to pause, breathe, and draw closer to God through guided moments of reflection, Scripture, prayer, and rest. Whether you’re overwhelmed, overthinking, navigating relationships, or simply longing for God’s peace, each episode offers gentle encouragement to help you slow down, quiet the noise, and remember His presence in the middle of everyday life. Come as you are, take a deep breath, and let’s rest in Him together.

title: The Relational Secret Sauce: We are better together
author: Brian and Emily Jones
description:
The Relational Secret Sauce: We are better together with Brian & Emily Jones


Most couples think they have a communication problem. Usually, they have a needs problem — buried under everything that piles up faster than anyone can name it.


We're Brian and Emily. He's a licensed therapist and the creator of the FERNS Method™; she's a relational coach. We're also married — which means we're not talking about this work from the outside.

Every week we sit down and talk honestly about what actually happens between two people: the fights that were never about the thing they were about, the stories we quietly build about each other, and what it takes to stay on the same side. Sometimes we bring in other couples and ask them the question we're always asking — what's your secret sauce? — because the couples who make it work are usually running the method without ever having heard of it.


No hot takes. No red flags. No gender wars. Just two people who chose each other, talking with people who chose each other, about how the choosing actually works.


🌿 Learn the full FERNS Method inside Connected Conversations → https://connected-conversations.thinkific.com/ 


Educational content only — not therapy, and not a substitute for care with a licensed professional in your area.

description:
God From the Flawed is a podcast for people with faith, doubts, questions… or all three at once.
Hosted by Jason Schmidt, this show dives into the hard conversations many people think about but rarely say out loud. Why does suffering exist? Why does God feel distant sometimes? How can faith survive in a world filled with pain, tragedy, fear, and uncertainty?
This isn’t a polished sermon from someone claiming to have all the answers. It’s honest conversation from a flawed person searching for meaning, wrestling with belief, and trying to understand God while standing in the middle of real life.

title: The Short Hop
author: Andrew Schmertz
description: *The Short Hop* , hosted by Andrew Schmertz, CEO and Founder of Hopscotch Go, explores the future of regional, on-demand air travel and the people, ideas, and innovations reshaping how we move. Through conversations with aviation leaders, entrepreneurs, travelers, and changemakers, Andrew brings listeners inside a new era of mobility where safe, efficient, flexible, and affordable air transportation makes distance feel smaller. Inspired by Hopscotch Air’s mission to connect people to the places and moments that matter most, *The Short Hop* highlights how regional airports, smarter travel options, and customer-centered service can help more people save time, avoid unnecessary ground delays, and get closer to where they need to be. From business trips and weekend escapes to the broader transformation of private and regional aviation, this series makes air travel more accessible, practical, and personal than ever before.

Each episode of Plastic Surgery Consults cuts through the noise on trending procedures, patient safety, and what really happens from consultation to recovery. Whether you're a med student, a medical professional, or simply curious about going under the knife, this is the conversation you've been waiting for.

author: Commander's Corner
description: Welcome to the show. Hosted by “Commander” Bill, this podcast explores the ideas shaping our world, from technology and business to cybersecurity, faith, and beyond. Each episode brings thoughtful conversations, practical insights, and a dose of curiosity about both the future and the past. author: Casey Elkins, Mark Stimpson
description:

Each week, the hosts and guests on the show reveal the latest developments in the financial world to keep you informed of changes that may affect you personally, while sharing some common sense strategies that will help you make the most out of your retirement savings. We are focused on helping investors preserve and grow their assets, increase their income, and reduce taxes.]

What would you like your retirement to look like and how would you like your assets to support that vision? Mark and Casey can help define objectives and establish a direction for your planning.

Mark Stimpson is a Managing Partner of Elevated Financial Management & Owner of Mark A. Stimpson and Associates Inc. in Federal Way, WA. For nearly 40 years Mark’s mission has been to bring Clarity, Confidence, & Independence to the retirement planning process.

Casey Elkins is a founder and managing partner of Elevated Financial Management and Owner of Elevated Retirement Planning. He believes knowledge is power and strives to educate clients in regards to financial literacy and their personal financial options.

For more information or to get your own questions answered, call or text (866) 668-3625.

title: Nuestro Campeón Podcast
author: Javier Pérez

Hosted by Abogado Javier Pérez, the Nuestro Campeón Podcast is a bilingual show featuring campeones—leaders, advocates, and vecinos making a meaningful difference in Dallas and beyond.

I'm singer-songwriter Jay Koostix, and I didn't learn to sing, play guitar, or make music until I was almost 30 years old. Like many people, I thought it was too late to start. Now I want to show you that it's not too late for you either.

My goal is to help you enjoy the journey of learning to sing, grow as a musician, and discover all things music—while adding a little fun, joy, and positivity to your life along the way.

description:
Stillpoints is a series of short contemplations held within immersive soundscapes.
Each episode features a reflection from a guest voice on a timeless theme, accompanied by original music from Robot Koch. The words open a door, the music creates space around them.
Designed for moments of presence, reflection, and deeper listening, these episodes invite you to slow down and reconnect with yourself, others, and the world around you.
Created by Kathi Hendrick and Robot Koch.

True stories the official record left in the dark, read to you one file at a time.
Nikola Tesla and the eighty trunks the government took the night he died. George Washington and the four musket balls that passed through his coat and missed the man inside it. Nixon and the thirty-seven hundred hours of tape he made of himself. Each episode of The Unsealed Archives is a short, true history of a real person the textbooks had no room for, researched and written by your host, EKO. New files opened weekly. Start anywhere. Every book is free to read at books.ekolovesyou.com.

Conversations about science, history, philosophy, technology, culture, and the human condition.

title: What's Your Weird?
author: Griffin
description:
Welcome to What's Your Weird? A Show-and-Tell for Your Next Obsession. 

Everyone deserves a place where they feel accepted, supported, and loved—and for many of us, those places are the communities built around the things we're passionate about. 

Join host Griffin as he explores the stories, communities, and fascinating rabbit holes behind the hobbies, fandoms, interests, and pursuits that bring people together. 

Together, we'll discover how people found these passions, what keeps them coming back, and the friendships and communities that grow along the way. 

You might find your next hobby. You might find your next obsession. At the very least, you'll meet some fascinating people along the way. 

So tell us... 

What's your weird?

title: The SPG Podcast

description: SPG is a dentist-first DSO that employs a paradigm-shifting de novo growth strategy to efficiently create unparalleled opportunities our doctors and teams to render transformational patient care in the dentures and implants sector. Our company exists to empower our doctors to fulfill our company purpose: to democratize the fixed arch for our patients. Dentists in our company receive the training and support (both clinical and nonclinical) to execute the types of dentistry that truly change our patients' lives.

title: Death is my Day Job
author: Maria De Jesus
description:
Death Is My Day Job is a candid, compassionate exploration of what it means to work in the world of aging, dementia, and end‑of‑life support. Hosted by Maria Rodríguez, a nurse leader and geriatric care advocate, this show pulls back the curtain on the emotional, cultural, and human realities behind Alzheimer’s care, senior advocacy, and the systems that support families in crisis.
Through honest conversations, real‑world experiences, and community‑based insights, Maria brings listeners into the spaces where aging, grief, resilience, and connection intersect. This is a podcast for caregivers,

title: Trophy Life: The Blueprint for How Former Female Athletes Win In Life After Sports
author: Alyssa Hermann
description:
*Free Private Podcast Series for former female athletes who are ready to stop chasing success and start feeling like they've won again.*
Former female athletes are often told that their athlete mindset is all they need to succeed after sport.
And while that mindset gives you the discipline, resilience, and drive to accomplish almost anything... it doesn't teach you *what* is actually worth pursuing, how to define success on your own terms, or how to build a life that feels as fulfilling as it looks.
Inside this private podcast series, Alyssa Hermann, RN, board-certified coach, and former soccer player, unpacks why so many former female athletes build successful lives after sport yet still feel disconnected from them, the hidden ways athletics continue shaping your decisions long after competition ends, and what it actually takes to channel your athlete mindset into a life that feels like winning again.
You'll learn about *The Gap* , why working harder has never been the solution, which parts of your athlete mindset deserve to come with you, and how you can apply a proven framework to channel your elite level ambition without sacrificing your fulfillment in the process.

title: Revive Functional Medicine
author: Melissa Bunkers
description: Melissa Bunkers, MSN, FNP-BC, is the founder and CEO of Revive Functional Medicine. She is a board certified Family Nurse Practitioner and certified Functional Medicine Practitioner with nearly 15 years of experience managing complex health issues. Revive Functional Medicine exists so that smart women (like you) can finally get *real* answers and solutions to your frustrating health issues that come from hypothyroidism and other hormonal imbalances. We're a team of certified Functional Medicine practitioners who will be behind you the whole way to educate, empower, and inspire you to get well and stay well. Through the latest lab testing (DUTCH, stool, micronutrient, full thyroid panel, and more), personalized root cause analysis, and high-level support, it's time to say goodbye to the confusion and hopelessness and instead start living your life with energy, clarity, and confidence that YOU are in control of your health.

We talk about the story, the obsession, and what it can teach us.  Story Obsessed is the podcast where passionate fans find their community.

title: Leaders of Today: From Teens to Titans
author: Lorraine Connell
description:
Welcome to Leaders of Today: From Teens to Titans, the podcast where we dive into the experiences of current Titan leaders to uncover how their teenage years shaped their paths to success and how a coach might have helped them along the way. Hosted by Lorraine Connell, Teen Leadership Coach and founder of Peers Not Fears, this show offers parents, teens, and aspiring leaders invaluable insights and practical advice.

In each episode, we candidly talk with leaders who reflect on their challenges, triumphs, and pivotal moments from their youth. We explore how mentorship and coaching helped or could have impacted their journey, and we invite them to become mentors for our program teens, sharing their wisdom and experiences.

Whether you’re a parent seeking to empower your child, a teen navigating the complexities of adolescence, or an aspiring leader looking for guidance, Leaders of Today: From Teens to Titans provides the tools and inspiration needed to cultivate leadership skills, foster resilience, and embrace potential.

Join our community dedicated to nurturing the next generation of leaders. Subscribe now and embark on a transformative journey towards unlocking your teen’s leadership potential!

title: Noise Complaint | The Stranger
author: Noisy Creek Podcasts
description: Noise Complaint is The Stranger's audio and video music podcast featuring live performances with Seattle's most exciting performers. Streamed live and released every other Friday, we invite a great band, musician, or wonderfully weird act to perform. Tune in, discover something new, and support art in your city.

title: Free Inside
author: Jeff G
description:
Free Inside is a weekly conversation with Jeff Garmire and Allison Powell about endurance sports, adventure, science, coaching, curiosity, and whatever else happened that week.
Some episodes dive into ultrarunning, hiking, and training. Others end up about bears, the World Cup, nonprofit work, overthinking, or why Jeff still doesn't know what he's wearing to the wedding.
No guests. No script. Just honest conversations, bad jokes, and the things we'd probably be talking about anyway.

title: Mugs & Medicine
author: Jessica McCartin
description:
Welcome to Mugs & Medicine, a health and wellness podcast hosted by Jessica McCartin, MSN, APRN, FNP-C, founder of Elite Primary Care & Wellness Clinic. Based in New Albany, Indiana, Jessica serves patients throughout Southern Indiana and the Louisville, Kentucky area through a personalized approach to healthcare known as Direct Primary Care (DPC).


Each episode explores practical ways to improve your health, navigate today's healthcare system, and make informed decisions about your well-being. From understanding what to expect during routine checkups to discussing preventive care, chronic disease management, nutrition, and lifestyle habits, Jessica provides straightforward guidance grounded in her experience as a family nurse practitioner.


You'll also learn how Direct Primary Care offers a different healthcare experience—one focused on accessibility, longer appointments, stronger patient-provider relationships, and transparent pricing without the constraints of traditional insurance models.


Grab your favorite mug, settle in, and join Jessica for conversations designed to help you take charge of your health—one cup and one episode at a time.

Where quantum physics meets consciousness, where neuroscience meets ancient wisdom, and where real human experience meets things we don’t yet have words for. No crystals required. No blind faith needed. Just honest, raw conversations about what it actually means to wake up to a bigger version of reality and yourself.

author: Clock Tower Media
description:
Throughout history, people have imagined what the future might look like — sometimes in bold visions, sometimes in whimsical predictions. Dreams of a Future Past is a podcast that explores how humanity has dreamed of the future through movies, technology, philosophy, and design.

With an upbeat, conversational tone, the show will dive into iconic dreams of the future — from concept cars and world’s fairs, to sci-fi films and technology — and ask: what came true, what never did, and why? Along the way, listeners will discover the stories of inventors, businesspeople, and dreamers who helped shape our imagination of tomorrow.

title: 2 Minutes of Guts | Sales Coaching
author: Jason Hartz: Sales Tips
description:
What if 2 minutes could change your life? 
I believe it can.
Over the years I've coached business leaders to home builders on how to take bold action. To be successful in any of these careers there's one common truth:
You might work a 50 hour work week, but you need 2 minutes of guts to close business.
I started this podcast to discuss the courage and the risk taking required to remain resilient when things get difficult.
I'm no expert podcaster (yet!), but I'm learning. I look forward to sharing these moments of real courage with you. It would mean the world if you listen, subscribe and share this journey with me.
This podcast is all about those 2 minutes- the moments of courage that change everything. We'll explore the science, psychology, and real-life stories behind risk taking, resilience, and trusting your instincts.

Join us each week as we explore God's Word, share biblical teaching, and discuss practical ways to grow in your Christian faith. Through inspiring sermons, faith-filled conversations, discipleship insights, and real-life testimonies, Echoes of Hope seeks to encourage believers, strengthen spiritual growth, and point listeners toward the hope found in Jesus Christ.

Hosted by two estheticians, Brenda Hogsett, Director of Player Experience, and Kelly Wolcott, Director of Judges, these candid conversations feature founders, competitors, judges, educators, ambassadors, and industry leaders. SPIRIT OF INTIMACY BROADCAST
Spirit of Intimacy Broadcast exists to teach you that intimacy with the Holy Spirit is not something you struggle to earn. It is a promise already given, and with the right understanding, it can become a daily, living experience.
Through simple, Spirit-led teachings, Pastor Courage helps believers understand Person and ministry of the Holy Spirit and guides them into learning how to walk with Him in the reality of His presence daily. 
NEW EPISODES ARE AVAILABLE EVERY TUESDAYS AND FRIDAYS .

title: Surf and Sales
author: Richard Harris & Scott Leese
description:
Everyone is looking for help in sales, yet so few are willing to give it unselfishly.
Richard Harris and Scott Leese have teamed up to bring their best advice from their own life experiences, real-world job experiences, and what they see sales reps and leaders do right and, equally, do wrong so you can be the best at your craft as well.
And yeah, there will be the occasional surf story.

title: The Iliad by Homer
author: Gates of Imagination
description:
Sing, goddess, the wrath of Achilles.

For nine long years, the armies of Greece have besieged the walls of Troy. But when a bitter quarrel erupts between King Agamemnon and Achilles, the greatest of all warriors, rage threatens to destroy the Greeks from within. As Achilles withdraws from battle, the tide of war turns – and gods and mortals alike are drawn into a conflict where honor demands blood, and glory comes at a terrible price.

Homer's Iliad stands at the very beginning of Western literature: a monumental epic of war, pride, grief, and the fleeting nature of human life. From the duel of Paris and Menelaus to the death of Hector before the gates of Troy, its scenes have echoed through three millennia – and lost none of their power.

This audiobook presents Samuel Butler's celebrated prose translation, prized for its clarity and directness, which renders the ancient epic vivid and accessible to the modern listener.

A timeless story of anger and reconciliation, fate and free will – and of what it truly costs to be remembered forever.

title: Time management, Accountability, Goal Setting, Self Improvement, Small Business : Your Productivity Pulse
author: Michelle Oucharek-Deo
description:
Your minutes matter. Every single one of them. And this podcast exists to help you use them with intention, energy, and a spark of joy.
Your Productivity Pulse is where time management meets real life. Whether you are a small business owner trying to get more done without burning out, an entrepreneur building something meaningful between the demands of everyday life, or someone who simply knows there has to be a better way to move through your days, this is your space. Hosted by Michelle Oucharek-Deo, a productivity coach, creative arts therapist, and business entrepreneur with over 30 years of experience, each episode blends practical strategy with genuine human insight to help you stop waiting for the perfect moment and start making the most of the one you are in.
This show goes beyond the checklist. Through her Time Core Strategy System, Michelle explores the real reasons we stall, scroll, and put our own goals last, and she offers grounded, sustainable strategies to shift that. From accountability and goal setting to mindset and self improvement, every episode is built around one belief: productivity is not about doing more. It is about doing what matters, in a way that actually fits your life.
If you have ever asked yourself any of these questions, you are in the right place:
How can I be more productive without working longer hours? Why am I so exhausted even when I feel busy? How do I stop procrastinating and actually follow through? Why do I keep putting my own goals last? How do I build a realistic schedule that I can stick to? How can I get more done and still have energy left at the end of the day? How do I take control of my time instead of letting it run me? How can I use my 168 hours differently this week?
These questions only scratch the surface. But they are exactly the right place to start. Pull up a chair, hit play, and let's figure this out together. Because the life you want to be living is already waiting for the time you are about to reclaim.

author: Koto IO
description:
The rules have changed. Technology at its best doesn't just change how we work. It changes what we are capable of.
Every once in a generation, a shift occurs that redefines the boundaries of human potential. We are living in that moment. Life in the Age of AI is not a podcast about algorithms, code, or complex systems. It’s a podcast about you. It’s about the profound ways artificial intelligence is quietly reshaping our world.
In each episode, we explore how artificial intelligence is dismantling the old barriers between your ideas and reality.

description:
Ce n'est pas un podcast.

Welcome to Stronger Than the Shift!


If you’re married to a firefighter, first responder, or someone whose career means missed holidays, sleepless nights, solo parenting, and learning to carry more than you ever expected—you’ve found your people.


I’m your host, Emily, and each week I’ll sit down with firefighter spouses and other first responder partners for honest conversations about the realities of this life. Together we’ll talk about marriage, motherhood, mental health, identity, resilience, shift work, and everything in between.


This isn’t a podcast about having all the answers. It’s about creating a space where we can share our stories, laugh together, cry together, and remind one another that we’re never as alone as this life can sometimes make us feel.


Whether you’re brand new to the first responder world or you’ve been living this life for decades, my hope is that every episode leaves you feeling a little more understood, a little more encouraged, and a little stronger than the shift.

The name is deliberate. Influence here is not "this person uses our product." It is communities, authenticity, and the people who genuinely shape how an industry thinks and buys. That is what we go deep on.

title: Rebecca Has Spoken | Christian Relationship Advice for Women | Boundaries | Confidence | Emotional Healing
author: Rebecca West
description:
Did you smell the smoke but still got burned by the fire?

Did you ignore red flags, second-guess yourself, give too much benefit of the doubt, stay too long or talk yourself out of what your gut - or your Mama - was trying to tell you?

I’m Rebecca, and mistakes and I are on a first name basis. I’ve smelled the smoke and still walked straight into the fire more times than I’d like to admit. I’ve had to crawl myself out of the pit of regret one bloodied knuckle at a time, holding onto Jesus with one hand and the therapist He sent me with the other.

If you’ve ever found yourself asking, “How did I get here?” you’re not alone.

Maybe you trusted the wrong person. Maybe you overcommitted at work. Maybe you stayed too long in a relationship, friendship, marriage or family situation because you believed that being loving, patient, forgiving or loyal would eventually change the outcome.

One of the biggest lessons I’ve learned is this: Healthy principles don’t produce healthy results when they’re applied to unhealthy people.

On this podcast, we'll have kitchen table conversations rooted in compassion without coddling, because healing doesn't come from staying comfortable. Together, we’ll have honest conversations about discernment, boundaries, relationships, faith, self-worth, people pleasing, codependency and learning to recognize unhealthy patterns – not just in other people, but in ourselves.

My goal isn’t just to help you heal from yesterday. It’s to help you develop the discernment to make better decisions tomorrow. Because once you learn to recognize unhealthy patterns, you stop repeating painful lessons and start building a life you don’t have to recover from.

Welcome to Rebecca Has Spoken.

Because painful lessons don’t define us – they refine us.

title: Guide to Mail-Order Plant Nurseries
author: harryjones
description: When ordering from a mail order plant nursery, it is best to know some of the factors involved that may impact your overall experience and success rate. First, find out what USDA zone you live in, and know that the plants you wish to plant will thrive in your climate; even plants with good health will be unsuccessful if not suited to your zone. Then check the nursery's shipping schedule because many mail order plant nurseries will not ship right away after they are ordered, but instead, will do so when it is best for the plant. Delivery times for mail-order plant nurseries will vary by season and depending on when you ordered the plants. Please read the nursery's return policies carefully and ensure that you know what you will be entitled to if plants arrive damaged or don't thrive. It also lets you get your garden bed or containers ready beforehand so you can plant as soon as you receive your order – live material performs best when transplanted quickly. Last but not least, be aware of the tracking details and make sure to be there on the date they are expected to arrive so they do not have to endure unnecessary stress before they are planted.
